title: Rebecca Has Spoken | Christian Relationship Advice for Women | Boundaries | Confidence | Emotional Healing
author: Rebecca West
description:
Did you smell the smoke but still got burned by the fire?

Did you ignore red flags, second-guess yourself, give too much benefit of the doubt, stay too long or talk yourself out of what your gut - or your Mama - was trying to tell you?

I’m Rebecca, and mistakes and I are on a first name basis. I’ve smelled the smoke and still walked straight into the fire more times than I’d like to admit. I’ve had to crawl myself out of the pit of regret one bloodied knuckle at a time, holding onto Jesus with one hand and the therapist He sent me with the other.

If you’ve ever found yourself asking, “How did I get here?” you’re not alone.

Maybe you trusted the wrong person. Maybe you overcommitted at work. Maybe you stayed too long in a relationship, friendship, marriage or family situation because you believed that being loving, patient, forgiving or loyal would eventually change the outcome.

One of the biggest lessons I’ve learned is this: Healthy principles don’t produce healthy results when they’re applied to unhealthy people.

On this podcast, we'll have kitchen table conversations rooted in compassion without coddling, because healing doesn't come from staying comfortable. Together, we’ll have honest conversations about discernment, boundaries, relationships, faith, self-worth, people pleasing, codependency and learning to recognize unhealthy patterns – not just in other people, but in ourselves.

My goal isn’t just to help you heal from yesterday. It’s to help you develop the discernment to make better decisions tomorrow. Because once you learn to recognize unhealthy patterns, you stop repeating painful lessons and start building a life you don’t have to recover from.

Welcome to Rebecca Has Spoken.

Because painful lessons don’t define us – they refine us.

title: Guide to Mail-Order Plant Nurseries
author: harryjones
description: When ordering from a mail order plant nursery, it is best to know some of the factors involved that may impact your overall experience and success rate. First, find out what USDA zone you live in, and know that the plants you wish to plant will thrive in your climate; even plants with good health will be unsuccessful if not suited to your zone. Then check the nursery's shipping schedule because many mail order plant nurseries will not ship right away after they are ordered, but instead, will do so when it is best for the plant. Delivery times for mail-order plant nurseries will vary by season and depending on when you ordered the plants. Please read the nursery's return policies carefully and ensure that you know what you will be entitled to if plants arrive damaged or don't thrive. It also lets you get your garden bed or containers ready beforehand so you can plant as soon as you receive your order – live material performs best when transplanted quickly. Last but not least, be aware of the tracking details and make sure to be there on the date they are expected to arrive so they do not have to endure unnecessary stress before they are planted.

title: The Grow Up Everyday
author: Reginald  Murray
description:
The Grow Up Everyday, Empowering Your Journey to Become 1% Better Every Single Day.

Welcome to The Grow Up Everyday podcast! This is your go-to audio source for personal growth, mental clarity, and lifestyle change. If you feel stuck, constantly fight procrastination, or seek the motivation to pursue your dreams, you have found your safe space.

We live in a fast-paced world where it is easy to lose focus, feel overwhelmed, and push our personal goals aside. We often know our goals, but we struggle with how to reach them. That is exactly where this podcast comes in.

The Grow Up Everyday aims to connect inspiration with action. We do not promote shallow motivational quotes or complex theories. Instead, we explore realistic, science-backed, and practical strategies that you can easily add to your daily routine starting today.

Here is what you can expect from our weekly episodes:

Crushing Procrastination: Proven methods to overcome laziness, build momentum, and stay disciplined, even when motivation dips.

Productivity & Time Management: Smart tips to improve your daily routine, manage your energy, and accomplish more in less time.

Mindset & Mental Well-being: Real conversations on boosting self-confidence, managing stress, overcoming self-doubt, and fostering a positive growth mindset.

Curated Wisdom: Actionable insights from life-changing books, philosophy, and real success stories condensed into easy lessons.

Whether you are a student striving to ace your exams, a freelancer building your career, or someone looking to bring more peace and structure into your life, this podcast is here for you.

It is time to stop waiting for the "perfect moment" and start creating it. Put on your headphones, hit play, and let’s improve your life together. True growth requires daily commitment, and remember, we Grow Up Everyday!

Subscribe and follow now to join a community of action-takers and never miss a step in your self-improvement journey.

description:
Welcome to the CMMC Proof Podcast, strategically commanded by Derrich Phillips, a battle-hardened cybersecurity veteran, Certified CMMC Assessor, and Provisional Instructor. 

Our mission: to navigate the complex battleground of Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC), with a tactical focus on the unique challenges and breakthrough strategies within the academies of higher education.

Each episode is a strategic operation, featuring elite conversations with the foremost commanders in cybersecurity – CIOs, CTOs, and CISOs from prestigious research universities.
 We strategize on crucial topics, including:

- Maneuvering through the intricate terrains of CMMC and NIST SP 800-171 compliance in the academic sector.

- Decoding encrypted messages of best practices and lessons from the cybersecurity trenches in higher education.

- Outflanking cyber threats to protect sensitive research data and intellectual property.

- Deploying AI and cutting-edge tech for fortified cybersecurity defenses.

At the CMMC Proof Podcast, our goal is to transform compliance from a battlefield into a training ground for cybersecurity excellence in academia. We equip university leaders and IT warriors with the intelligence and arsenal needed to turn cybersecurity challenges into opportunities for victory and innovation.

Join us in the CMMC Proof Podcast for mission-critical insights and actionable intelligence that prepare you for a future where cybersecurity is the stronghold of academic and research success.

Salute to a future where cybersecurity compliance is your university's shield and spear in the digital age.

title: AI-Naama
author: Wadhwani AI
description:
Wadhwani AI was founded on the conviction that technology must serve the underserved. AI-Naama — meaning "a register of AI" or "an account of AI" in Urdu and Hindi — extends that mission into the public sphere. It is a podcast series by Wadhwani AI that brings rigorous, accessible discourse about AI and subsequent digital public goods to a broad public audience.


AI systems are increasingly shaping consequential decisions in health, agriculture, education, credit, and governance – potentially in ways that could be opaque, inequitable, or unaccountable. These risks are especially acute in the Global South, where regulatory frameworks are nascent, affected communities are underrepresented in the design of these systems, and the stakes of getting AI wrong are highest.


AI-Naama convenes government, researchers, practitioners, policymakers, and civil society voices to examine what responsible, ethical AI actually looks like in practice - not as an abstract ideal, but as a contested, urgent, and evolving project.

title: In This Moment with Toby C.
author: Mutual Broadcasting
description:
Helping Open Hearts and Minds in a World of Overstimulation Life moves fast. Stress, anxiety, addiction, unhealthy relationships, fear, and loneliness can leave us feeling overwhelmed and disconnected. In This Moment with Toby C. invites listeners to slow down, take a breath, and discover that lasting recovery and personal transformation begin by becoming fully present. Hosted by longtime recovery advocate Toby C., the program draws upon the enduring wisdom of the Twelve Steps while presenting universal principles that apply to anyone seeking a healthier, more meaningful life. Although addiction recovery is often the starting point for the conversation, the message reaches far beyond alcohol and drugs. Every episode speaks to the human experience—our need for connection, purpose, faith, humility, forgiveness, and hope. Listeners are encouraged to move beyond simply surviving and begin truly living through practical discussions about: 
•	Recovery and emotional healing 
•	Overcoming fear, shame, and isolation 
•	Healthy relationships and personal growth 
•	Spiritual principles for everyday living 
•	Sponsorship, service, and helping others 
•	Finding purpose through faith and community 
•	Living one day—and one moment—at a time 
The program isn't about perfection. It's about progress. It's about discovering that every setback can become a stepping stone toward a better future. Whether you're beginning your own recovery journey, supporting a loved one, or simply searching for greater peace and direction, In This Moment with Toby C. offers a message of encouragement that resonates with listeners everywhere. Recovery is possible. Hope is real. Your next chapter can begin right now... in this moment.

title: Lighthouses
author: Richard L Burr
description:
Every episode is one lighthouse. Every lighthouse has a story.
Some of them are about the people who kept the lights burning — alone, for decades, through storms that took everything else. The woman who held the lamp through a month-long gale while the sea swept her house away. The keeper who rowed into the harbor, again and again, because that was the job. The man who climbed eighty-seven steps every day for thirty years after anyone stopped paying him to.
Some of them are about the structures themselves — what it actually took to build something that could stand where the ocean was trying, specifically, to kill it. What happened on the days it lost.
Each episode runs about two minutes. One lighthouse, one story, one reason it earned its place.
See the light.

Each week, we are diving deep into the everyday anxieties of pet ownership. We aren't just talking about basic training; we are tackling the real stuff:

  • The Overthinking: From choosing the right food to understanding why they hate grooming.
  • The Science: Speaking to veterinary behaviorists to decode pet anxiety and weird behaviors.
  • The Psychology: Sitting down with human psychologists to explore how pets heal our mental health, help people with autism, and why an entire generation is choosing "fur babies" over actual babies.

Whether you're trying to break the cycle of household stress, looking for expert advice from people in the field, or just want to feel less alone in your pet-parenting panic, this show is for you.

description: What does it actually take to walk away from a good job? The Startup Conversation hosts real talks with people who left stable careers despite having —  mortgages, families, and every reason to stay — to build something of their own. No venture capital, no Silicon Valley, no gurus. Butchers, builders, coffee roasters, trade-school founders, mostly from in and around Lexington, Kentucky. Every episode follows the Startup Sequence, a six-stage arc from the life they left to the one they built — including Max Q, which is the moment the whole thing nearly came apart. Hosted by a guy with golden handcuffs of his own, asking the questions on behalf of everyone still deciding. New conversations every other Tuesday.

title: Training, Tumours & Trauma Podcast with Blake Austin
author: Blake Austin
description:
Life doesn't always go to plan. Injuries happen. Diagnoses happen. Setbacks happen. The question is: how do we keep moving forward?


Im Blake Austin, a personal trainer/coach, educator and survivor of a brain tumour, two brain surgeries, radiation treatment, two back surgeries and debilitating nerve pain. Through it all, I've learned that life is about giving back, paying the good forward and helping others live more fulfilling and rewarding lives. 


On Training, Tumours & Trauma, we have deep, honest and sometimes uncomfortable conversations about training, mindset, nutrition, music, movies, hobbies, gaming, resilience, recovery and the challenges that shape us. Whether you're chasing a fitness goal, rebuilding after an illness and/or injury, navigating lifestyle obstacles, or simple looking to become a stronger, healthier version of yourself, this is the podcast for you.

title: Ask the (Employee) Advocate
author: Patrick C. Daniel
description:
Ask the (Employee) Advocate is the podcast for leaders who understand that the toughest business challenges are rarely about policies—they're about people.

Hosted by executive advisor, mediator, and former Chief Human Resources Officer Patrick C. Daniel, each episode explores the leadership decisions that shape organizational culture, influence performance, and determine long-term success. Drawing on more than 35 years of executive experience, Patrick offers practical insights on workplace conflict, executive judgment, employee relations, organizational change, governance, and leadership resilience.

Whether you're a CEO, board member, HR executive, manager, entrepreneur, or emerging leader, Ask the (Employee) Advocate delivers thoughtful conversations and real-world strategies to help you navigate complexity, make better decisions, and lead with confidence.

Because conflict is rarely the problem—it's usually the symptom.

Ask the Advocate is a production of The Focused Approach, LLC.

title: Know Your Game podcast
author: by USIndoor
description:
Welcome to the Know Your Game Podcast from the US Indoor Sports Association where we discuss ideas, experiences, products and services that can help your indoor sports business thrive! Be sure to like and subscribe if this is your business (or would like it to be) and join us as we learn! 
Memberships at USIndoor are on sale for your first 90 days for just $1, just head to usindoor.com to learn more! 
Be sure to check out our podcast series and if you'd like to take part in a future episode, you can reach out to James Hargrove, Communications Director at james@usindoor.com.

title: Understanding life
author: Charla
description: About uplifting  goals staying positive

title: Plotopsy: Dissecting Stories
author: Allie James
description: Every story has an anatomy. On Plotopsy, Allie James sits down with authors to dissect the stories behind their books. Through thoughtful conversations about inspiration, worldbuilding, characters, themes, and the writing process, each episode explores what makes a story work. Featuring traditionally published and indie authors across a wide range of genres.

title: 電子タバコは何歳から吸える？法律とマナー、健康リスクを徹底解説
author: lin lin
description:
「成人年齢が18歳に引き下げられたけれど、電子タバコは何歳からOKなの？」という疑問を抱く方は少なくありません。特に最近は、スタイリッシュなデザインや多彩なフレーバーの登場により、若い世代の間でもVAPE（ベイプ）への関心が高まっています。今回は、2026年現在の最新状況に基づき、電子タバコの利用年齢や法的な位置付けについて詳しく解説します。
まず、基本的な定義として、 電子タバコとは？ ( https://blog.kyotovape.com/vape-beginner-guide/ ) という点を確認しておきましょう。これはタバコ葉を燃焼させるのではなく、食品添加物などにも使われる成分と香料を混ぜた「リキッド」を電気で加熱し、その蒸気を吸い込むデバイスを指します。一方、アイコス（IQOS）などの「加熱式タバコ」はタバコ葉を使用するため、法律上は20歳未満の喫煙が厳格に禁止されています。
では、ニコチンを含まない電子タバコ（VAPE）はどうでしょうか。法的な解釈では、ニコチンなしのリキッドはタバコ製品に分類されないため、 電子タバコ 未成年者喫煙禁止法 ( https://blog.kyotovape.com/vape-underage-law-risk/ ) （現在の二十歳未満ノ喫煙ノ禁止ニ関スル法律）の直接的な対象外となる場合があります。しかし、ここが重要なポイントですが、日本国内の主要メーカーや販売店、コンビニ各社では、青少年の健康保護と喫煙習慣の誘発防止を目的として、独自の「自主規制」を敷いています。そのため、実店舗やオンラインショップの多くでは、購入時に20歳以上であることを確認する年齢認証が必須となっています。
利用を検討する際に避けて通れないのが、「 電子タバコ 害はあるのか ( https://blog.kyotovape.com/vape-health-risks/ ) 」という問題です。ニコチンやタールが含まれていないとはいえ、蒸気を直接肺に吸い込む行為が身体に与える長期的な影響については、現在も世界中で研究が進められている段階です。特に成長期にある若年層にとっては、呼吸器への刺激や未知の成分による健康リスクが懸念されるため、安易な使用は推奨されません。
こうした複雑な年齢制限の背景や、正しいデバイスの選び方、そして2026年の最新トレンド情報を網羅的に発信しているのが、 KYOTO VAPE 電子タバコ 最新情報ガイド ( https://blog.kyotovape.com/ ) です。
結論として、電子タバコは「法的なグレーゾーンはあるものの、20歳になってから楽しむもの」というのが社会的な共通認識であり、マナーです。自分自身の健康を守り、ルールを守ってスマートにVAPEライフをスタートさせるための準備を整えましょう。

title: Terra Incognita
author: Paolo Ottaviani
description:
Terra Incognita explores the untold stories of tech and innovation across emerging markets.
Each episode takes you on a journey through six countries that are rewriting the rules of innovation, far from the usual Silicon Valley spotlight. We tell the stories of the people, the numbers, and the ideas building the future where most people are not looking.
For investors, founders, policy professionals, and anyone curious about what comes next. The companion Substack gives you the full picture, with data and deep dives.

title: Schmidt to Cintas
author: Jene Galvin
description: Welcome to Schmidt to Cintas, conversations about Xavier University's rich basketball success, hosted by two guys who've been there from 1961 to today Jene Galvin and Leo Bradley.

title: The Alvarez Hypothesis
author: Javier Alvarez
description:
What if the greatest experiment you’ll ever run is your own life?


The Alvarez Hypothesis explores one central question:


What happens when ordinary people fully commit to living according to God’s design?


Hosted by Javier, this podcast dives into the intersection of faith, family, leadership, business, discipleship, and everyday life. Each episode examines biblical principles, practical wisdom, and personal experiences that challenge listeners to reject passive living and intentionally build a life that glorifies Christ.


This isn’t about chasing comfort, success, or status.


It’s about forging character, leading your family well, pursuing excellence in your work, building meaningful relationships, and leaving a legacy that points others to Jesus.


Whether you’re a husband, wife, parent, entrepreneur, young professional, or someone simply seeking a deeper purpose, you’ll find conversations that encourage you to think critically, live courageously, and follow Christ faithfully.


Together, we’ll test one hypothesis:


A life surrendered to God is the strongest foundation for a meaningful life, a thriving family, and an enduring legacy.


Faith. Family. Fellowship. Legacy.


Welcome to The Alvarez Hypothesis.

title: The Family Biz Show
author: Michael Palumbos
description:
Join host Michael Palumbos and new guests every episode as they talk about everything from navigating family business transitions, wealth transition, business growth strategies, family conflict, leadership and team development and more. 
Don't forget to share your favorite episodes with others. Tag us with #thefamilybizshow!
If you're a family business or a family business consultant and want to be on the show, share your story and help other family businesses, send us an email to producer@thefamilybizshow.com or visit us at The Family Biz Show | Family Business Podcast With Michael Palumbos (familywealthandlegacy.com) to fill out our web form! 
Securities and investment advisory services offered through Osaic Wealth, Inc. member FINRA/SIPC. Osaic Wealth is separately owned and other entities and/or marketing names, products or services referenced here are independent of Osaic Wealth.

title: Humans are Forever with Dr. Ravi Gundlapalli
author: Dr. Ravi Gundlapalli
description:
Hosted by entrepreneur, innovator, and technology visionary Dr. Ravi Gundlapalli, each episode brings together leading thinkers, scientists, entrepreneurs, educators, and changemakers to examine the evolving relationship between human intelligence and machine intelligence. Through compelling conversations and real-world stories, the show explores how AI is transforming the way we work, create, learn, heal, and connect while asking what will always remain uniquely human.
Join Dr. Ravi Gundlapalli as he explores the future of human intelligence, one conversation at a time, and discovers why, no matter how advanced technology becomes, Humans are Forever.

title: Special Education News and Noteworthy
author: Inclusive Educational Practices
description: Special Education News & Noteworthy is a weekly podcast that breaks down what’s happening in the world of special education—across policy, funding, legal decisions, and community impact. Hosted by Paige Fitzgerald and Kate Magaldi, founders of Inclusive Educational Practices, each episode makes national headlines accessible and actionable for parents, educators, advocates, and anyone who cares about inclusive education.

title: The FAFO Podcast
author: FAFO Podcast
description:
FAFO Podcast Facts. Truth. No BS. This isn’t a safe space—it’s a real space. The FAFO Podcast is where uncomfortable truths get said out loud, weak excuses get exposed, and reality hits exactly the way it’s supposed to. We talk about life, culture, failure, accountability, and the consequences nobody wants to admit exist. No scripts. No filters. No hand-holding. If you’re looking for validation, keep scrolling. If you’re ready for the truth—welcome in. You f*** around… you find out.

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/the-fafo-podcast--7183608/support.

title: OSHOISOME
author: sarthak bhagat
description:
Welcome to "OSHOISOME".

Here, we share the wisdom and unique vision of life that Osho brought to the world—not through closed beliefs, but through open eyes, awareness, and understanding.

Join us on a journey of self-discovery, mindfulness, and deeper insights into life. Explore Osho's teachings, timeless truths, and perspectives that encourage us to live consciously, question freely, and see the world with clarity.

Open your eyes. Open your mind. Discover yourself.

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/oshoisome--7182815/support.

title: The WELL at College & Dickson Podcast
author: The WELL at College and Dickson
description:
Previously titled: FBCFAYCOLLEGE Podcast
Conversations with Josh and Alison to help students know, trust, love, worship, follow, and obey Jesus more.

title: Wait… What was I gonna say? with Sarah
author: sarahwillmon
description: “wait…what was I gonna say? with sarah” is for anyone whose brain has 37 tabs open and at least one is playing music. Hosted by Sarah: your chaotic, overthinking, oversharing new bestie—this podcast is full of unfiltered rambles, pop culture drama, vivid (and sometimes unhinged) dreams, personal stories, and random tangents that make you forget what you were even talking about. It’s funny, it’s real, it’s messy—grab a snack, get cozy, and let’s spiral.

title: Life in Fife
author: Time Capsule Productions
description:
Life in Fife is an online archive and podcast documenting Fife and all its people from ex-miners to artists and everything in between.
Fife has so much to offer and there are so many great stories in this Kingdom that have never been told, until now. These are businesses, charities, creatives, community groups, and individuals with a story to tell.
Each episode is like a mini documentary creatively produced with the interviews and archive content that have been edited into shorter, enjoyable and easy-to-listen-to episodes.
I’ll record local anniversaries, discussions about former industries and local creatives, musicians and artists. I'll be talking to local businesses, from someone's side hustle to high street names. As well as shining a light on the charities and community groups helping Fife's people in the background. Everyone has stories and unique skills that are valuable to share.
Preventing the loss of local knowledge, highlighting groups that people can get help from or volunteer with and connecting local communities.

title: Yoga with Ramoni Podcast
author: Yoga with Ramoni
description: Yoga is more than what happens on the mat. The Yoga With Ramoni Podcast takes the practice deeper — into relationships, emotions, patterns, and real life. Hosted by yoga innovator and wellness educator Ramoni Overton, each episode connects ancient yoga philosophy to the things you're actually going through.

title: Issues & Challenges In Sterile Processing a Podcast Conversation
author: Jason Marosi
description: A podcast conversation about the problems SPD professionals face every day: Instrument reprocessing, sterilization standards, staffing pressure, leadership challenges, workflow breakdowns, infection prevention, and patient safety.

title: Soul Stories with R.R. Shakti
author: R.R. Shakti, PhD
description: Stories, teachings and reflections from the world of Applied Mythology. R.R. Shakti explores the symbols, archetypes and timeless questions that shape a creative life.

title: Let’s Talk Truths
author: Morgan and Marley
description:
Calling all tweens and teens! Led by BFFs Morgan and Marley, this podcast talks about everything from school to self care. Listen in to hear two girls’ real experiences and thoughts, as well as tips and tricks.
Contact us at LetsTalkTruths863@outlook.com

title: Book Keeper with Megan Phelps-Roper
author: Longview
description: Megan Phelps-Roper was raised inside one of the most infamous religious communities in America. More than a decade after she left, and now a mother herself, she's returning to the Bible — not to reclaim belief, but to ask a harder question: What’s worth keeping?

title: Leaving Well: nonprofit leadership guidance for workplace exits and transitions
author: Naomi Hattaway
description: This is Leaving Well, where we talk about the reality that People Leave™️ in nonprofits and the social impact sector. Through this podcast, you will receive expert insights on leadership exits and transitions, the benefits of interim leadership, and sustainable succession planning in nonprofits. Listen to learn transition strategies for executive director, CEO, and board of directors leadership during resignations, terminations, and unfortunate circumstances such as death.

title: Spine Time
author: Nebraska Spine + Pain Center
description: Your back does a lot for you. It's about time you returned the favor. Spine Time brings physicians, surgeons, physical therapists, and healthcare professionals together for honest conversations about one of the most misunderstood parts of the human body: your back and neck. We bust common myths, answer the questions patients are too nervous to ask in the exam room, and talk about what staying healthy looks like right here in Nebraska.

title: MOVIAA
author: Jean Lordeus
description:
MOVIAA = Men Of Valor In Action Always
The MOVIAA Podcast's goal is to enliven thought-provoking topics and Anecdotes that have fallen by the wayside due to life's busyness. 
The aim is to help someone regain consciousness once more and live. 
MOVIAA will cover a gamut of social issues, Spiritual Revival, Financial Literacy, Self-Worth Awareness, and Uplifting and Promoting young men to their rightful place in this society.

title: The Hallowed Raven
author: Hallowed Raven LLC
description: The Wounded Spark Saga is an upcoming gritty, gothic-horror actual play podcast of D&D

title: Built To Win - a SigEp Podcast
author: Sigma Phi Epsilon Fraternity (SigEp)
description:
Winning conversations with the men who built their success the right way.
Built to Win, hosted by Seth Irby, sits down with Sigma Phi Epsilon's most accomplished alumni — Fortune 500 CEOs, industry pioneers, and world-renowned physicians — for the real stories and real playbooks behind elite success.
Guests include Bob Eckert (former CEO of Mattel and Kraft Foods), Terry Lundgren (former Chairman & CEO of Macy's), Jeff Henley (Vice Chairman of Oracle), Dr. Jay Harness (internationally recognized breast cancer surgeon), and more.

title: The Banner with Avery Elise
author: Avery Elise
description: In a world that wants to label you, lift up your banner that tells everyone you have already won the battles you're facing because of Jesus Christ! This podcast gives you big sis advice in hopes to take you from vision to victory. This is "The Banner with Avery Elise."

title: The Severed Thread
author: fromtheashespromotions
description: Avoidant discard isn’t just “they left and didn’t look back.” It’s a whole pattern that trains you to abandon yourself to keep the connection, while they run from their own feelings and call it peace. In this episode we look at both sides of avoidant discard, why it hurts so much, and what it actually takes to start healing instead of chasing.

title: 'EHM All Access
author: Harry Wareing
description: EHM All Access brings you inside the 'EHM Studio with artist interviews, festival moments, and behind‑the‑scenes stories. Hosted by Harry Wareing, it’s real conversations and real access to the events, artists and music you love. Get it wherever you get your podcasts.

title: Hela Show
author: Hela
description: Hela Show is an unfiltered conversation about the patterns we live, the lessons we almost missed, and the soul work underneath it all — hosted by Hela, nervous system specialist and founder of the HELA Method.

title: Honestly Speaking
author: India Grace
description: Let's get HONEST about life and mental health.Hosted by a psychology student and coming from the perspective of real everyday people, we are talking love, society, consent, religion, personal development and so much more. Come and join the discussion where nothing is off limits. Hosted by India Grace.

title: Medical Class 901 Podcast
author: Betty LaMarr
description:
Medical Class 901 Podcast, hosted by award-winning Memphis Business Journal's healthcare hero Dr. Chinelo Animalu, MD, MPH, FACP, FIDSA,  Professor of Medicine and HIV specialist, addresses critical issues surrounding infectious diseases that impact the younger generation in Memphis, Tennessee, and globally. 


This podcast places a key emphasis on sexually transmitted diseases (STDs) affecting individuals aged 14 to 24. STDs can be devastating and potentially deadly if left untreated. 


Dr. Chinelo Animalu, MD, will provide an in-depth exploration of the various sexually transmitted diseases that are plaguing our communities.

title: Housing Compass: A Navigate Gives Back Podcast
author: Navigate Affordable Housing Partners
description:
Housing Compass is a Navigate Gives Back podcast from Navigate Affordable Housing Partners.
In Season 1, Community Partners, Community Impact, we talk with nonprofit partners working to meet critical needs, support housing stability, and strengthen communities.
Launching August 3, this season features conversations about clean water access, food support, home repair, community revitalization, and housing with dignity.

title: The Other Ireland
author: Nick F
description:
The official community for The Other Ireland podcast.


A place to share and discuss Irish folklore, supernatural encounters, forgotten history, local legends, and the unexplained stories passed down through generations.


Have you heard a strange tale from your town? A family story? A local legend or an unexplained experience?


Share it here and help keep The Other Ireland alive.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: A little bit about us
author: Emilly Villamizar
description: This podcast consists of a small part of our lives and what we want.

title: The Shelf Life of Business
author: Aadhya Guntupalli
description: Join us as we dive into the pages of top business books and unpack real-world examples of marketing, economics, and leadership. Whether you're an aspiring entrepreneur or just curious about what makes successful companies work, tune in for fresh perspectives and actionable insights, all through the lens of a high school student.

title: Area of Effect - A TTRPG Show for Parents & Educators
author: The Kids Table Games LLC
description: Did you know that playing D&D can have just as many benefits for families and students as playing sports? Area of Effect is a show where we discuss how tabletop roleplaying games benefit the whole family. As former educators, Ryan and Allison interview experts like TTRPG game designers, educators, psychologists, and more as we give tips to help you bring the adventures to your own table.

title: Streetz Talk podcast
author: Alfie D
description:
Streetz Talk Podcast is a platform for authentic dialogue and community building. We dive deep into the male experience through raw conversations about self-knowledge, life challenges, and aspirations. Join us as we create a space for open expression, share insights, and inspire personal growth.
From heartfelt discussions to electrifying special performances and informative interviews, we bring the energy of the streets to your ears. Don't miss our signature segment, "Sunday's Dinner," where knowledge is always on the menu and thought-provoking convers

title: The Freeway Faith Podcast
author: Freeway Faith
description:
Freeway Faith is a mother-and-son podcast where Gen X and Gen Z perspectives meet at the intersection of faith and everyday life.
Join Heather and Riley as they explore Scripture, ask honest questions, share life experiences, and discuss what it means to follow Jesus in today's world. From Bible study and church history to culture, family, gaming, and unexpected side quests, no topic is off limits.
Pull up a seat, buckle up, and join the conversation. Keep your eyes on the road and your heart on the Word.

title: Who's a good human?
author: Sian Gard
description:
Who's a good human is a podcast all about the relationships humans have with animals.


From cats and dogs, to birds, cows and sheep. Who's a good human is a collection of stories about animals, told by the people that have loved them.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Aggie Effect Podcast
author: Aggie Athletic Foundation
description: The Aggie Effect Podcast is the official podcast of the Aggie Athletic Foundation, celebrating the people, stories, and traditions that strengthen North Carolina A&T State University and Aggie Athletics.  Through engaging conversations with alumni, student-athletes, coaches, university leaders, donors, and community partners, the podcast explores how Aggies are creating excellence, making an impact, and building a lasting legacy.  Each episode highlights the experiences, achievements, and personal journeys that reflect the spirit of Aggie Pride while connecting listeners to the mission of the Aggie Athletic Foundation. From leadership and athletics to philanthropy, service, and the rich history of North Carolina A&T, The Aggie Effect Podcast shares the stories that inspire our community and advance the future of Aggie Athletics.

title: My Texas Ballot
author: Andee Kinzy
description:
What if every voter worked their way through their own ballot before Election Day?
My Texas Ballot is a long-form interview series hosted by Andee Kinzy exploring the people and offices on her Texas ballot. Each episode digs into what the office actually does, why it matters, and the experience each guest brings to public service.
Whether you're preparing to vote or simply want to better understand how local government works, My Texas Ballot goes beyond campaign headlines to help you hear directly from the people on your ballot.
New episodes are released as conversations happen. Follow so you don't miss one.

title: The Women I Worry About.
author: Maggie Lamle
description: The women I worry about is for the women. Everyone depends on the women carrying more than anyone realizes hosted by Maggie Lamle, a former ER and ICU nurse this podcast, explores burnout, resilience, faith, healing, loss, purpose, and what happens when the strong ones finally tell the truth through honest conversations and personal stories you'll find perspective Hope and the reminder that you were never meant to carry everything alone.

title: Late Night Waves
author: Rod Waveeee
description:
Late Night Waves is a podcast for people who feel everything deeply.
If you relate to Rod Wave music, late-night thoughts, heartbreak, and real-life struggles… this is your place.
We talk about pain, growth, loneliness, and turning emotions into strength. Every episode is real, honest, and made for people who don’t always say what they feel — but carry it inside.
This is more than just a podcast.
It’s a feeling.

title: Fireproof Your Money
author: Wayne Firebaugh
description: You've been marketed to your whole life. This show fights back. Hosted by Wayne Firebaugh, CPA and CFP® — a genuine fiduciary who has to put your interests first — Fireproof Your Money busts a costly retirement myth each episode and shows the 50-and-over crowd how to sidestep the preventable mistakes that quietly torch a retirement, from overpaid taxes to high-pressure annuity pitches. Smart, a little mischievous, and always in your corner.

title: Utopia Decision Room
author: Gina Kimiza Tan
description: Utopia Decision Room is a podcast about building a software company in the US — the calls, the pivots, and the decisions founders usually make alone. Every week, I sit down with one founder and unpack one decision that changed how they run their business. Hosted by Utopia Club.

title: The Front Line
author: Pennsylvania Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
description:
Join us on the front line for The Front Line Podcast
Whether you are a veteran, service member, military family member, or a dedicated advocate, this podcast speaks directly to your experience. We sit down with influential leaders across the commonwealth who are on the front lines fighting to improve the lives of those who served. So, march into the fray with us as we stand shoulder-to-shoulder, navigating the high-speed operational tempo of military and veterans affairs.

title: THE BEFORE AND BEYOND PODCAST.
author: Oluwanifesimi Akilapa
description:
Welcome to the Before and Beyond Podcast.
We are here to talk real stories- from who we were to who we are becoming. 
This is the space where the past meets with purpose. 
Grab a seat, you are about to get transformed.
Subscribe now to join the family.
You can send an email to thebeforeandbeyondpodcast@gmail.com for enquiries.
For paid partnerships, you can send an email to thebeforeandbeyondpodcast@gmail.com
If you will like to give your life to Christ, you can send an email to thebeforeandbeyondpodcast@gmail.com for guidance. 
Thank you and welcome once again.

title: Daily Bread
author: Emmanuel John
description:
Daily Bread is a short daily Bible podcast created to bring peace, hope, and encouragement through scripture, reflection, and prayer.
Each episode features calming Bible readings, thoughtful devotional messages, and simple prayers designed to strengthen your faith and help you stay connected to God throughout the day.
Whether you are starting your morning, ending your evening, or searching for peace in difficult moments, Daily Bread is here to help you slow down, reflect, and rest in God’s presence.
New episodes released daily.

title: Own Your Revival
author: Andy Arbie
description: Welcome to the Own Your Revival podcast hosted by Andy Arbie, Catholic Life Coach, for faithful women who know the pathway to more peace, joy and purpose is found when we take radical responsibility for ourselves.   Because when we do, we step into the fullness of who we were made to be by a God who loves us a lot.   Join us every week as we keep it real and have fun sharing trials, tips and testimony to grow from where we are to where God is calling us to be.

title: Crazy Brit Asian
author: Chris Mak
description: What's up ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the Crazy Brit Asian Podcast where we document the nuances of the British East Asian experience. That means talking about a wide range of topics around all things Asian, not just in the UK but beyond! It's an ever evolving area of conversation so if you can think about it, we're probably going to talk about it. Each episode we delve into a conversational cultural exploration with a guest because who better to talk about something than someone who's lived it? Real people, real talk, real life experiences IG: https://www.instagram.com/crazybritasian

title: The Brisbane Bullets Podcast
author: Ollie Godden
description: Hosted by NBL Royalty and Bullets captain Mitch Norton, The Brisbane Bullets Podcast goes under the bonnet to bring you closer to the action on the court and all the stories off it.

title: Sad satan
author: Nicolás Dagostino
description: paranormal

title: Dannie B Speaks...The Podcast
author: Dannie B Speaks
description: This podcast is an extension of my former website danniebspeaks.com where any and everything is discussed.

title: Fractured But Fearless
author: Dr. S. Irfan Ali
description:
Fractured but Fearless is a podcast about the journey most leaders never talk about.
Dr. Irfan Ali came to America with only $1,700. He cleaned carpets at night while attending medical school during the day. 
Through discipline, risk, and resilience, he built one of the largest privately held physician groups in West Florida.
On this podcast, he sits down with founders, physicians, entrepreneurs, artists, and executives who are willing to speak honestly about:
    • The failures that shaped them
    • ﻿﻿The losses that forced growth
    • ﻿﻿The resilience required when no one is watching
    • ﻿﻿The risks no one sees
    • ﻿﻿The turning points that changed everything

title: Building One Piece at a Time
author: Patriot Talk 920 AM
description:
Building One Piece at a Time is an honest journey of faith, family, and entrepreneurship.

I'm Matt Velasquez—a husband, father, Christian, and owner of Patriot Talk 920 AM in Houston. After losing both of my parents, I was left with the responsibility of carrying on our family's 40-year radio legacy. Instead of staying with what was familiar, I took a leap of faith and completely reinvented our family business.


This podcast is my honest look at what happens after you take that leap—the wins, the losses, the mistakes, the financial struggles, the relationships, and the lessons I'm learning along the way.

I don't have all the answers, and this isn't a story being told from the finish line. I'm still in the middle of it, trying to trust God's plan, honor my family's legacy, and build something that can make a lasting difference in my hometown of Houston.


Because the things that matter most aren't built overnight. They're built one piece at a time.

description:
Life doesn’t always go as planned. We get stressed. We react. We miss the exit. And sometimes… we forget to be kind to ourselves along the way.


Welcome to Mindful, Kindful Living — a podcast for people who want to slow down, laugh at their humanity, and live with more mindfulness and compassion.

I’m Connie Warden, mindfulness teacher and author of Speeding to Compassion, and each week I sit down with thoughtful guests to explore real-life stories — the messy moments, the turning points, and the small shifts that help us move from reaction to reflection.


This podcast is for you if you:

  • feel the pressure to be perfect
  • know that your health is your wealth.
  • want to respond to life with more calm and clarity
  • are curious about mindfulness in everyday situations
  • or simply want to feel a little more at ease being human


We talk about things like emotional intelligence, stress, relationships, resilience, and self-compassion — all through the lens of real stories and honest conversation, with just enough humor to keep things light.

New episodes drop weekly on Wednesdays and each one is an invitation to take a breath, reset, and gently steer back toward what matters.


If that sounds like something you need, hit follow, and come join us in Mindful, Kindful Living.

Category: File this under "Kids & Family" — this is the standard category Spotify and Apple Podcasts use, and it's what determines whether you show up in kids-specific browsing/charts, so it's worth getting right rather than defaulting to something broader like "Stories" or "Arts."

Episode title formula — worth using this consistently, since individual episode titles are searched/indexed too, not just the show title:



Ep 1: Flo and the Dragon's Egg | Magical Flo Kids Story

Cover art specs (this trips a lot of people up): design at 3000×3000px, square, JPEG format, kept under 512KB file size — Apple Podcasts enforces this strictly even though you're publishing via Spotify, since most distributors push to both platforms from one asset. Bold, simple text and a clear character (Flo's face works well) reads better at the tiny thumbnail sizes podcast art actually gets seen at.

One thing worth deciding now: since your show name has a 100-character limit on Spotify, and you'll want each episode title to also carry good keywords, it's worth keeping the show title itself fairly lean (like above) and letting each episode's individual title do more of the specific-keyword work — that way you're not trying to cram everything into one title field.

well a bit of a mix


Reconciled contextual ambiguity and revised content description accordingly

title: The Gifted PLaCe
author: Sonu Aziz
description:
About The Gifted PLaCe


Connecting Research, Educators, and Practice to Advance Understanding of Neurodiverse Learners


The Gifted PLaCe was created to bring together researchers, educators, and practitioners to explore current research, emerging ideas, and meaningful practices that support neurodiverse learners. Drawing on her experience as an educator, researcher, and scholar-practitioner, Dr. Aziz brings together conversations with leaders in the field to explore topics related to gifted and advanced learners, twice- and thrice-exceptional students, curriculum and instruction, educator development, and the evolving landscape of education. The Gifted PLaCe aims to bridge research and practice by creating opportunities for educators to engage with new perspectives, reflect on their work, and consider approaches that support the academic, social, and emotional development of learners with diverse strengths, abilities, and ways of experiencing the world.


About Dr. Sonu Aziz


Dr. Sonu Aziz is an educator, researcher, and scholar-practitioner whose work bridges gifted education, curriculum development, educator preparation, and educational research.
As Head of Academic Affairs at Long Island School for the Gifted, Dr. Aziz supports curriculum development, faculty learning, and instructional practices across grades K–8. Her work focuses on creating responsive learning environments that challenge and support learners while helping educators develop the knowledge, skills, and perspectives needed to meet the needs of diverse learners.


Dr. Aziz holds a Certificate in Advanced Gifted Education, and her scholarly interests center on gifted learners, including twice- and thrice-exceptional students, and the ways educational systems can better recognize and develop diverse expressions of ability. Through her doctoral research in Leadership & Innovation at New York University, which examined teacher perceptions of twice-exceptional students and received the Distinguished Problem of Practice Award, Dr. Aziz explores how research can inform practices that strengthen identification, instruction, and support for neurodiverse learners. Her engagement with the field extends beyond her research and school-based work through her service on the Public Policy and Advocacy Committee for the National Association for Gifted Children and her contributions to GiftedNYS.


Her work is grounded in a commitment to connecting research with practice and helping educators create learning environments where students feel challenged, supported, and valued. In recognition of her contributions to gifted education, Dr. Aziz received the Dr. Rosemary Callard-Szulgit Student Award for Gifted Education in 2026.


Through The Gifted PLaCe, Dr. Aziz continues to create opportunities for educators and researchers to engage in thoughtful conversations that deepen understanding of neurodiverse learners and translate research into meaningful educational practice. Her work reflects a commitment to helping educators recognize student potential, develop responsive approaches, and create learning environments where students’ strengths and pathways for growth are understood and supported.
