author: Tatiana Smolina & Arjun Rull
description:
We're Tatiana & Arjun, and we just launched your new favourite audio podcast!
🍷🥃 It's busy, it's authentic and it's just.. life!
We started this podcast out of curiosity, a lot of mutual respect, and a love for deep, unfiltered conversations. For us, no topic is too small, and no perspective is off-limits.
Consider this your official invite into the beginnings of our judgment-free zone. We’re here to question the norm, explore everyday topics through a fresh lens, and focus on what brings us all together rather than what drives us apart. We’re learning as we go, and we want you to join us!

title: Maximizing Your End-of-Service Benefits in the UAE
author: Usman Amir
description: Navigating employment benefits can be challenging, especially when it comes to understanding gratuity payments. In this episode, we discuss how UAE employees can better understand their end-of-service entitlements, avoid common calculation mistakes, and prepare for important career transitions. We also highlight how a Gratuity Calculator UAE https://gratuitycalculatoruae.ae/ can provide quick estimates based on salary and years of service, helping workers gain clarity about their potential benefits. Tune in for practical insights that can help you make smarter financial decisions throughout your professional journey in the UAE.

title: Witness to Success
author: Troy Korsgaden
description: Troy Korsgaden shares his exclusive conversations with the most successful players in the insurance industry and beyond. Learn from the success stories of field leaders, industry icons and the upcoming pioneers. Troy has witnessed some of the best in action and now brings you exclusive access to their stories.

title: The Premier Foundation Repair Podcast
author: Garfield
description:
The Premier Foundation Repair Podcast brings homeowners practical, trustworthy guidance from Julie Miller, President of Premier Foundation Repair. Each episode helps you understand the subtle—and sometimes not‑so‑subtle—signals your home sends when its foundation needs attention. From cracks in the brick to interior drywall shifts and stubborn doors that refuse to close, Julie explains what these signs mean, why they matter, and how to address them with confidence. 


With more than 35 years of experience serving DFW homeowners, Premier Foundation Repair is known for fixing foundation problems right the first time. This podcast blends expertise, clarity, and real‑world insight to help you protect your home, avoid costly surprises, and stay ahead of issues before they grow. If you want straight talk, reliable information, and a foundation expert in your corner, you’re in the right place. 

 

To learn more about Premier Foundation Repair visit:
https://www.PremierFoundationRepair.com/
Premier Foundation Repair  
2810 E Trinity Mills, Suite 209-292 

Carrollton, TX 75006 

972-417-0823

title: Queen B: Grace, Grit and Reinvention
author: Steve Koch
description: Success is never a straight line.  Before the luxury real estate, before the billion-dollar business, before the recognition, Valerie Fitzgerald was a single mother with no safety net, no business experience, and no roadmap—just a determination to create a better future.  Queen B: Grace, Grit and Reinvention is a podcast about rebuilding your life, embracing change, and discovering what's possible when you refuse to quit on yourself.  Each week, Valerie shares the lessons she's learned through decades of building one of Los Angeles' most successful luxury real estate businesses, alongside honest conversations with entrepreneurs, business leaders, authors, and inspiring people who have navigated setbacks, reinvented themselves, and created extraordinary lives.  Together, they'll explore topics like:  Building confidence before you feel ready Reinventing yourself after life's biggest challenges Leadership, resilience, and personal growth Wealth, purpose, and creating opportunities Real estate as a pathway to freedom and long-term security The life lessons success can never teach on its own  Whether you're starting over, chasing a dream, navigating change, or simply looking for the courage to take the next step, this podcast is here to remind you that your story isn't defined by where you begin—it's shaped by the decisions you make along the way.  Because confidence isn't built before action.  Confidence is built through action.

title: Are You There Yet?
author: By Carmen & Anna
description:
Are You There Yet? is the conversation you have with your best friend when the coffee is gone, the masks are off, and nobody is pretending to have it all figured out.
Join us as we explore the questions, challenges, laughs, and lessons that come with being human. From relationships and family to healing, purpose, spirituality, and those unexpected detours life throws our way — we’re figuring it out one conversation at a time.
Because the truth is… none of us really knows exactly where we’re going.
But maybe the journey is the point.
So… are you there yet?

title: Socket 742: En
author: Marco Jimenez
description:
Do you feel like you arrived too late to artificial intelligence? You didn’t.
Socket 742 explores creativity, technology, and the human layer behind every project. Using the author’s real experiences, notes and past work, AI tools transform that material into dynamic conversations about learning, creating, and reviving ideas once left behind.
The raw material is human. The voice is digital. The purpose is not to replace human creativity, but to help us reconnect with what we have always wanted to build.
This is Socket 742. Plug into the conversation. Where human stories meet digital voices.

title: Salty Saints
author: Hayden Holm
description: Inspiring Latter-day Saint content, without the spirit voice or green jell-o, to strengthen your faith, family, and community. Join us as we navigate staying faithful in spite of the world, the culture, and social media. Tune in for debunkings, discussions on gospel topics, missionary stories, cultural callouts, and navigating questions faithfully.

title: Bowl of Data - The Podcast
author: Marco Parrillo
description:
Bowl of Data is a short weekly briefing on the tech that matters: AI, cybersecurity, blockchain, quantum computing, and software engineering. Every week, host Marco cuts through the noise to cover the stories, research, and model releases actually worth your time: no hype, no filler, just a direct, occasionally witty read for engineers, founders, and tech-savvy investors who don't have time for fifty newsletters. New episodes every week.
Website: https://www.bowlofdata.net/

title: Bloom to Become
author: Arya Hassan
description:
Bloom to Become is your go-to podcast for all things self-growth, wellness, and girl talk. Hosted by Arya Hassan, this podcast is like having comforting conversations with your older sister, or being on FaceTime with your best friend! 
Arya covers everything from building confidence and staying consistent, to navigating life experiences, habits, and finally becoming the person you want to be.
Come as you are, and grow at your own pace loves 🌺

title: English Conversation Hub
author: Lao
description:
Welcome to English Conversation Hub—your trusted place to learn real, natural, and confident English through engaging conversations and practical lessons.

Whether you're a beginner or an advanced learner, each episode is designed to improve your listening, speaking, vocabulary, pronunciation, and everyday communication skills. Learn authentic English used by native speakers in daily life, travel, work, and social situations.

Our mission is to help you speak English with confidence through easy-to-understand explanations, real-life dialogues, useful expressions, and interactive practice.

title: For Your Ears Only
author: Kay Camp
description:
Welcome to my personal audio diary!

On this pod, I talk about feelings, past relationships, being a parent, and just life in general!

title: The Graveyard Slot
author: The Graveyard Slot
description:
It's 3 a.m. and the station should be dead air. Instead, someone is reading you a story.

The Graveyard Slot is a horror fiction anthology broadcast from the loneliest shift on the dial. Each episode, your host takes the overnight desk and reads one complete tale... no cliffhangers, no filler. Original scary stories about small towns, empty highways, night shifts, trail cameras that see too much, and the quiet ways ordinary lives come apart.

Slow-burn dread over jump scares. Every story finishes before the sun comes up.

New episodes twice a week. Best heard alone, after midnight, with headphones. All stories are original works of fiction.

title: The Tech Society
author: The Tech Society
description: This is the Tech Society podcast hosted by Caden and co-host Alexis

title: The Reading Success Podcast
author: SOR Tutoring
description: The Reading Success Podcast by SOR Tutoring, featuring insights from https://www.sor-tutoring.com, helps K-8 students overcome reading and writing challenges with expert online tutoring guidance, literacy tips, and dyslexia support.

title: Tiny Cosm
author: Jasmine
description: Tiny Cosm explores psychology, astrology, subconscious reprogramming, meditation, nervous system healing, and the journey of becoming your higher self.

title: American Regret
author: American Regret
description:
Smart people don't ruin their lives by accident. They do it by being convincing — and the person they convince is themselves.

American Regret is a forensic examination of how capable, self-aware people talk themselves into the choices that end their lives as they knew them — and how the rest of us are doing the exact same thing right now, just with smaller stakes and no federal agents involved yet.

Every episode takes one person who had everything to lose and lost it: the cancer researcher who trusted the wrong person, the dealer who cooperated with the DEA for six years and called it bad luck, the Border Patrol agent who refused to cooperate and did the time. We don't tell their stories for the shock. We dissect them for the pattern — the rationalization, the language people use on themselves, the four seconds of hearing yourself clearly that separates the people who stayed free from the ones who didn't.

This isn't true crime and it isn't self-help. There's no healing arc, no five steps, no redemption on a schedule. Just the unflinching architecture of self-deception, examined by someone who built her own and did her time taking it apart.

Your host is Deana Martin — the Fairy GodFelon. She's not a doctor or a coach. She's the smartest person who was ever wrong about everything, finally telling the truth. New episodes every two weeks.

Listen close. You're going to recognize someone. The problem is it's going to be you.

title: Hello, Little Dreamer- Kids Sleep Stories
author: Melissa and Clair Brown
description:
Hello, Little Dreamer is designed to help kids wind down, regulate their big feelings, and drift to sleep feeling safe, worthy and loved.
Each episode gently weaves:
• Emotional intelligence and resilience skills
• Age-appropriate coping tools
• Kind inner dialogue
• Family connection themes
• Light, comforting fantasy elements
• Slow pacing for real nervous system calm
These aren’t fast, flashy stories. They are intentional, soothing bedtime journeys that help children process their day, understand their feelings, and build confidence from the inside out.
Parents can expect stories that:
• Normalize big emotions like frustration, jealousy, sadness, and worry
• Model healthy communication and repair
• Teach regulation tools like grounding, breathwork, body awareness, and perspective-taking
• Reinforce belonging, teamwork, and unconditional love
• Encourage independence and self-trust
• Close gently, without abrupt endings or stimulation spikes
At the heart of every episode is one core message:
You are safe. You are loved. You are learning.
Our Values
We believe:
• Children deserve emotional education woven into everyday life.
• Parents deserve support, not shame.
• Imagination is a powerful tool for resilience.
• Bedtime can be connection, not conflict.
And most importantly:
Hello, Little Dreamer will always remain ad-free.
No interruptions.
No sponsored messages.
No products inserted into your child’s wind-down routine.
Your child’s nervous system is not for sale.
This podcast exists to create a calm, consistent bedtime ritual you can trust.
So snuggle in.
Dim the lights.
Take a slow breath.
And let’s begin…
“Hello, Little Dreamer…”

title: Leaning Into The FIRE: couples therapy conversations
author: Ed Peterson
description:
LEANING INTO THE FIRE: couples therapy conversation is a podcast about the real work of couples therapy—what actually happens in the room when relationships are struggling and people are trying to change. Hosted by Ed Peterson, LCSW and EFT Supervisor, the show explores the emotional patterns that keep couples stuck and the moments that help them find each other again. Through thoughtful conversations about attachment, conflict, vulnerability, and repair, the podcast offers a window into how meaningful change actually happens in relationships.

Each episode reflects on the courage it takes to face hard conversations rather than avoid them. Drawing from Emotionally Focused Therapy, attachment science, and years of clinical experience, the podcast explores how couples move from blame and distance toward understanding, safety, and deeper connection. Episodes may include reflections on real therapy dynamics, discussions with other therapists, and practical insights that help people understand the emotional cycles that shape their relationships.

LEANING INTO THE FIRE: couples therapy conversation: is for therapists, couples, and anyone curious about the deeper emotional forces that drive love, conflict, and reconciliation. Rather than offering quick tips or surface-level advice, the podcast focuses on the deeper work of emotional healing—helping people understand their own fears, longings, and attachment needs so they can build stronger, more secure relationships. 🔥

title: SuperPod Saga
author: Aaron Klaassen
description: A chill podcast that discusses every video game topic in existence, one episode at a time.

title: Texas Trash Titans
author: Texas Trash Titans
description: At Texas Trash Titans, our approach to junk removal in Houston, TX is centered on efficiency, reliability, and customer care. We know that every cleanup project is unique, so we take the time to understand your needs and provide tailored solutions that fit your schedule and budget.

title: TRUCK
author: Alex Vespestad
description:
There's something about being in an old car. Is it loud? Yes. Does the AC work? Absolutely not. Are the seatbelts load-bearing? Debatable. But outside the window everything shifts to another place and time.


Podcast journalist Alex Vespestad picks up musicians in vintage rides and drives the California coast. They talk career highs, late nights, the stories that've never made it to an interview. And when the conversation reaches an end, they get in the back for a serenade under the open sky.


It's a feeling you've known your whole life...you've just never heard it like this.

title: The DubLine Podcast
author: The DubLine Podcast
description: Welcome to The DubLine Podcast, where sports, competition, and storytelling come together. Hosted by Kam Kaiser and Cole Schweda, the show features intense conversations, mini-games, and unique insights into the sporting world. From athlete stories and hot takes to behind-the-scenes experiences and friendly competition, each episode brings a fresh perspective on the games, people, and moments that make sports so captivating.

title: 六六社区
author: 六六社区
description:
小红书涨粉，小红书粉丝，小红书刷粉，小红书点赞
评论，收藏，转发，各种平台刷粉、买粉，小红书买粉

title: The Pinnacle: A Journal by Scholar-Practitioners
author: The Pinnacle: A Journal by Scholar-Practitioners
description: A podcast to accompany the journal.

title: Government's Future Frontiers
author: Deloitte Insights
description:
Justice. Security. Health. Technology. The on-the-ground realities of homelessness. The infinite possibilities of space.
These are all big issues – bigger than any one organization can handle. Truly tackling the problems and opportunities inherent in these big societal concerns will take a concerted effort from all walks of society – public agencies and private companies, non-profits and academics, multinational foundations and community activists. But no matter what teams are assembled, government will play a key role in constructing the scaffolding on which solutions are built.
Government's Future Frontiers will examine what it takes to confront some of the most pressing issues of today, and the emerging questions that will help build the future. We'll look at what it takes to build the coalitions of public and private sector players who can take on these issues, and share the solutions and ideas that are emerging from around the globe. Because we can't predict all the issues that will emerge in the coming years – but we can provide a roadmap to how those issues may be resolved.
Government's Future Frontiers is brought to you by Deloitte Insights and the Center for Government Insights.

title: Garden Spot Communities Presents: Purpose In Retirement
author: Garden Spot Communities
description:
We explore what it means to retire with purpose, to make a difference, to invest in your family and your community, to live to your full potential and explore abundant opportunities to live with purpose in community. Each episode features a Garden Spot Communities resident or team member and highlights the impact they are making on their world.
Purpose in Retirement is hosted by Scott Miller and Juanita Fox.

title: GameDay Garage: Campus Blitz
author: Ryan Horvat & PJ Glasser
description:
GameDay Garage: Campus Blitz, hosted by Ryan Horvat and PJ Glasser, is your home for college football coverage all season long. From preseason rankings and conference races to rivalry week, the College Football Playoff, and bowl season, Ryan and PJ break down the biggest games, championship contenders, Heisman races, futures markets, and the storylines that define every Saturday.

More than a traditional college football show, Campus Blitz combines expert analysis, audience interaction, betting insights, and live reactions into an alt-cast experience built for college football fans. Whether it's picking the biggest games of the week, identifying value in the betting markets, debating playoff contenders, or reacting to the chaos that only college football can deliver, Ryan and PJ bring fans inside the action every step of the way.

title: The Body Podcast
author: Stephanie Franzén
description: A scientific podcast about medicine, physiology and pathophysiology. Hosted by Associate Professor Stephanie Franzén PhD.

title: When We Gather Podcast
author: Kendria Waltower
description:
When We Gather is a faith-based podcast and Spirit-led space where healing, restoration, and transformation take place.
Created by Kendria Waltower, this podcast invites families and believers to pursue healthy, whole, and abundant lives through Scripture-rooted conversations led by the Holy Spirit. Each episode creates room for honest dialogue, wisdom, and reflection as God meets us in meaningful ways.
When We Gather exists to restore families, heal hearts, strengthen homes, and build the next generation of believers. This is a safe place where faith is stirred, wisdom is shared, and lives

title: Djembe Sessions
author: Djembe
description:
Welcome to Djembe Sessions, your go-to destination for smooth, soulful, and immersive deep house mixes. This platform is dedicated to lovers of deep house music and anyone who appreciates good music that speaks to the soul.

Each mix is carefully curated by DJ Djembe, blending rich rhythms, melodic grooves, and timeless sounds to create a listening experience that is both relaxing and uplifting. Whether you’re unwinding, driving, working, or setting the mood, Djembe Sessions delivers the perfect soundtrack.

If you love deep house, good vibes, and quality music selection, you’re in the right place.

Press play and let the music take over.

title: Sto Theo Me Paei
author: Eleni Foureira
description: Eleni Foureira

title: Intuitively Speaking
author: 90.3 WHPC
description: If you have questions, sometimes, the answers may come from a place you may never expect.  Real psychic readings, incredible personal stories, and mysterious connections.  Where the living have questions, and sometimes, the dead have the answers!  Sponsored by EdifyYourSoul.com.  Hosted by Psychic/Medium Laurie Smith.

title: The Church in Uganda Podcast
author: The Church in Uganda
description:
The Church in Uganda Podcast is a weekly Bible teaching ministry dedicated to proclaiming the true Gospel of Jesus Christ and equipping believers to live as faithful disciples in preparation for His return.

Hosted by Pastor Simon Peter Ebiu, each episode offers biblical teaching, spiritual encouragement, and practical discipleship rooted in the Scriptures. Our desire is to point people back to Christ, strengthen the Church, and inspire believers to walk in truth, holiness, and the power of the Holy Spirit.

In a time when many voices compete for our attention, this podcast seeks to faithfully present the unchanging Word of God—calling the Church to return to the simplicity of the Gospel, take up the cross daily, and follow Jesus wholeheartedly.

Whether you are a new believer, a church leader, or someone searching for biblical truth, you are welcome to join us each week as we grow together in God's Word.

New episodes every Wednesday.

"I will build My Church, and the gates of Hades shall not prevail against it." — Matthew 16:18

title: eCommerce, Clearly
author: Core Commerce Labs LLP
description:
eCommerce, Clearly is where I break down eCommerce reports, trends, and data — and explain what they actually mean for founders and operators. Most insights sound smart… but aren't actionable. Every episode, I cut through the noise to give you the so what: what the latest data, reports, and market shifts mean for how you build, scale, and make decisions. If you're building, scaling, or thinking about starting in eCommerce — especially in India's fast-moving market — this is for you. New episodes every week.

ecombeyonddropship.substack.com

title: AI & Human Insight
author: Martha Little
description:
Hey, I'm Martha Little, and I've been making podcasts for a long time. But this one feels different.
AI & Human Insight grew out of a question I just can't shake: now that we're living alongside powerful AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT, what's actually happening to our creativity, our curiosity, our empathy — the things that make us uniquely human?
I'm not here to panic about it. And I'm not here to hype it either. I'm genuinely curious.
So I'm doing what I do best — talking to people. AI founders building tools for creatives, marketers, filmmakers, advertisers — and the artists and everyday

title: Warrior Poet Society
author: Warrior Poet Society
description: Warrior Poet Society

title: Christ Church at Bell Creek
author: Christ Church at Bell Creek
description: By the Grace of God, our Father, with the Love of Our Lord Jesus Christ, along with the Communion of The Holy Spirit, we intend to be a Local Body of Believers that foster community and accountability while ministering to the spiritual needs of those in our fellowship, through Prayer, Worship, and ministering of the Holy Scripture and at the same time, becoming a beacon of light that adorns the darkness in our society with the light of the Gospel of Jesus Christ and the true orthodox faith.

title: Ritual Barbell Podcast
author: Shawn & Mark
description:
Short, digestible conversations about getting stronger, and living better because of it.


Ritual Barbell is a small, community-driven strength gym on the east side of Madison, WI. No mirrors. No TVs. Just a decade of serious, thoughtful coaching and a group of people genuinely motivated to build a better life through quality training. 


Coaches Shawn & Mark break down the program, answer real questions from the gym floor, and cut through the noise around fitness culture, all so you can train smarter, recover well, and actually enjoy the process. Whether you're a first-timer finding your footing or a seasoned lifter chasing your next PR, there's something here for you. 


Strength for life. Not for show.

title: The Economic Podcast for Gen Z
author: Stefan Bartl
description:
This is a show about thinking through the economic world we’re inheriting - together.
It’s built around conversations with Gen Z and young professionals who are already living with the consequences of big economic forces: inflation, housing, work, debt, technology, and policy decisions they didn’t design but will soon be responsible for.
No hot takes, no lectures - just careful dialogue about what’s happening and what it means.

title: We Are Still Here
author: Let's Talk PCS
description: Post Concussive Syndrome is often misunderstood, minimized, or dismissed. This solo podcast exists to shift that narrative. It explores the lived experience of PCS and chronic pain — from physical symptoms and emotional weight to invisible struggles and quiet resilience. There’s no medical advice here, just honest reflection, encouragement, and real-life perspective from someone navigating it firsthand. This space is for those living with PCS, recovering from it, or seeking to better understand it. Healing isn’t linear — it’s about learning how to rebuild your life around what remains.

title: Beyond the Ceremony
author: Jason Fellows
description:
Beyond the Ceremony is a podcast about what happens before, during, and long after the ceremony ends.


Hosted by Jason and Kelly Fellows of Tribal Detox, each episode explores the intersection of Kambo, Bufo, psychedelics, spirituality, personal growth, and the science of healing. Through honest conversations, interviews, and years of firsthand experience, we dive into the questions that don't always have simple answers.


You'll hear discussions on consciousness, trauma, integration, plant and amphibian medicines, nervous system health, ancient traditions, modern research, and what it means to live a more authentic life.

Whether you're completely new to this world or have spent years walking the path, Beyond the Ceremony offers thoughtful conversations grounded in curiosity, humility, and real-world experience.

New episodes released regularly.

title: Operation Advocacy: Voices for Children's Health
author: Operation Advocacy
description:
A podcast by pediatric surgeons with a mission to improve the lives (health) of children through simple conversations. Join children's health policy & advocacy topics with content experts. 

Episodes released monthly - tune in & join the conversation! 

Sponsored by the American Pediatric Surgical Association (APSA).

title: My Many Selves
author: International Web Marketers, LLC
description:
My Many Selves is a podcast dedicated to harmonizing the diverse parts of our identity—from the seeker and the builder to the healer and the dancer. By bringing these roles into orbit around our central essence, we can live more fully and create more freely, moving beyond the limited labels assigned by others to achieve true, whole-self integration.
Each episode explores the beautiful contradictions we carry through intimate stories, ancient wisdom, and modern tools. Whether discussing the medicine of scent or the intersection of technology and consciousness, we explore the gentle truth that we flourish when all our parts move in harmony rather than in conflict. If you are ready to stop hiding pieces of yourself and begin living authentically across all your dimensions, this is a space for deep remembering and exploration. Welcome home to My Many Selves: Becoming More You.
My Many Selves is hosted by Damaris Nightingale, an entrepreneur, researcher, and speaker whose work bridges consciousness, psychology, olfactory therapy, health, technology, and human development. After more than 30 years leading enterprise technology initiatives, her interdisciplinary approach integrates science, logic, and spirituality into a practical framework for deeper self-understanding.
Co-produced by Endiku LLC and WMR.FM. All Intellectual Property Owned by Endiku LLC.

title: Radio Renzhal
author: Donya
description:
رنژال برگشته؛ این بار با نگاهی تازه به غذا، سلامت و پیشگیری. از بشقاب شروع می‌کنیم و به بدن می‌رسیم؛ به قلب، مغز، روده و دوره‌های مختلف زندگی. توی این فصل، غذا نقطه شروع ماست، نه همه داستان. از خواب، فعالیت بدنی، استرس، فرهنگ و سبک زندگی حرف می‌زنیم؛ از چیزهایی که هر روز پشت انتخاب‌های غذایی ما ایستاده‌اند و آرام‌آرام مسیر سلامت ما رو می‌سازند.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Hero Ball
author: Colton Pool
description: Hero Ball is a weekly college football podcast, presented by HERO Sports and BetMGM. Senior writer and analyst Colton Pool is your host, and he brings on a plethora of guests who cover college football in the Group of Six conferences. The show features a variety of segments, from rankings to in-depth discussions, all with the goal of putting a spotlight on the G6.

title: T&A Podcast
author: The Legal Podcast Network (LPN)
description: T&A Podcast is a dynamic series hosted by two women with law degrees, Tara and Angela, who explore the intricate world of family law. The show offers listeners a blend of legal insight, practical advice, and relatable discussions on topics ranging from custody battles to divorce drama. With a focus on clarity and real-world application, the podcast aims to educate and empower individuals navigating the legal system. Whether you're dealing with co-parenting challenges or prenup debates, T&A Podcast provides a thoughtful perspective on the legal issues that matter most.

title: Echoes of the Line
author: The Mixed Museum
description: The railways are integral to the story of the British Empire and migration to Britain. Yet the multiracial history of Britain’s railways is often overlooked. Echoes of the Line from The Mixed Museum offers a fresh perspective, unearthing long-buried tales from archival research and oral history interviews that challenge our notions of who worked and travelled on the trains. Covering the period from the dawn of commercial rail travel in the 19th century to the present day, it offers a brand new take on our shared past. Visit www.mixedmuseum.org.uk to learn more.

title: Stranamore – Dispatches from Elsewhere
author: USMARADIO
description:
Strangelove – a strange love.
A love of the strange.
Love while estranged.
The bends (le stranie) that come with love and its depth.
Melancholy, alienation, loss. Surprise.
Belonging in between the lines, outside the margins.
Resistance.
Playing in face of defeat.

Curated by Clelia Ciardulli

Clelia Ciardulli is a radio broadcaster, soundscapist, and crucially, a “citizen of elsewhere.” Born in Bolzano, raised in Chiavenna (Italy), shaped by years in Sheffield and Glasgow, and now based in Berlin, her musical journey began with her mother’s record collection before moving onto the dancefloor. After learning the basics of DJing at Red Tape Studios in Sheffield, she found her creative home in projects exploring music as cultural encounter, from the DisOriental nights in Bradford to the DIY experimental scene of The Audacious Art Experiment in Sheffield. Immersed in punk, post-punk, noise and free improvisation, she discovered music as a way of creating spaces for listening, community and critical exchange beyond the conventions of the music industry. In 2014 she spoke at the Guelph Jazz Festival, connecting with the work of Ajay Heble on improvisation, collaboration and cultural practice. Stranamore is an extension of that journey: a sonic space for the strange, the irreverent, and the deeply human.

title: SB Talks
author: Stanford Brown
description: Insight and analysis on investment markets and issues affecting your whole financial world.

title: Narrative Engineering Studio
author: Olivier Le Blanc
description: Hosted by strategic communications specialist Olivier Le Blanc, The Narrative Engineer deconstructs how the world's most resilient public-sector agencies, NGOs, and enterprises build the infrastructure for institutional storytelling.

title: Deprogramming Occlusion - Bioesthetic Dentistry
author: OBI Foundation
description: Dr. David Maclean and MDT Bernhard Egger explore the world of Bioesthetic Dentistry, uncovering why modern restorative techniques often miss the mark by treating symptoms without exploring the "why." Dentists and Dental Technicians alike will benefit from OBI's unique and holistic approach to optimum dental health in this ongoing educational series sponsored by the OBI Foundation for Bioesthetic Dentistry.

title: Solace Overland: Stories from the Road
author: Solace Overland
description:
Every journey begins with a dream, but the best stories begin within.
Join Andrew as he travels across mountains, deserts, borders, and remote landscapes, sharing honest reflections on overlanding, adventure, fear, freedom, and the pursuit of a life filled with experiences rather than possessions.
From the driver's seat to some of the world's most unforgettable roads, each episode is a reminder that sometimes the greatest journey isn't about where you arrive, it's about who you become along the way.

title: The IGNITE Your Church™ Podcast
author: Nina
description:
Most churches don't have a passion problem. They have a clarity problem. Somewhere between the first-time guest and the fully-formed disciple, people are slipping through the cracks and most leaders can't see exactly where.


IGNITE Your Church Podcast exists to change that.


Each episode, host Nina Hampton, Church Communication Strategist at Text In Church, digs into one stage of the IGNITE framework: the six-stage roadmap that moves people from first visit to lasting impact. 


These are real conversations with real church leaders, industry experts, and people passionate about serving their church well to give you a clearer picture of where your church is actually losing ground.


Because passion gets you going. A roadmap gets you there.


Ready to find your church's biggest growth gap? Take the free IGNITE Scorecard at igniteyourchurch.scoreapp.com.

title: Paper Trail: Cold Case
author: Erick
description:
Behind every cold case is a paper trail that was ignored. Welcome to Paper Trail: Cold Case.

True crime isn’t just about the "who" or the "how"—it’s about the evidence left behind in the dark. This investigative true crime podcast bypasses the sensationalism to focus on the cold, hard facts. We dig deep into overlooked police logs, buried financial records, court transcripts, and bureaucratic cover-ups that the system wanted to stay hidden.

Each season of Paper Trail: Cold Case is an empathetic, document-driven deep dive into America's most haunting unsolved mysteries, missing persons files, and wrongful convictions. This is true crime with a purpose: we partner with victim advocates, legal experts, and families to hunt for accountability, ask the tough questions, and bring neglected archives back into the light.

Because a case is only "cold" if we choose to forget.

🔍 Join the Investigation: New episodes drop daily. Follow us to never miss an update.

title: Sustainably Dutch
author: Dan Broersma
description:
Welcome to Sustainably Dutch, where we discuss simplified sustainability for practical application and our perspectives and philosophy about sustainability.
With over 20 years of experience in sustainability, Dan spent more than 18 years at Herman Miller and is now working for the City of Holland's residents, helping maintain sustainability leadership through best practices and innovation. His passion for sustainability extends into his personal life. As a volunteer, he has led community projects by helping local schools, organizations, and businesses become sustainable by setting up better processes, sharing knowledge, and implementing best practices for better sustainable practices.

title: The InnerMission Podcast ❤️
author: Danielle Brooks
description: Hosted by Academy Award Nominee, actress, Danielle Brooks, The InnerMission Podcast is a stylized, intimate deeper-dive into the subconscious of today's leading creatives. We explore the beautiful, messy, and necessary in-between" moments when life forces the curtains closed. This series is an invitation for both the guest and the listener to log off, drop the armor, and uncover their actual inner mission when the static fades. No scripts, no stages, just pure human alignment.

title: EMERGENCY AWARENESS
author: Elizabeth Caldwell
description: Any news topic or general topic of utmost importance to the audience so they know how to handle the emergency.

title: Rethinking Wellness
author: Christy Harrison, MPH, RD, CEDS
description: Rethinking Wellness offers critical thinking and compassionate skepticism about wellness and diet culture, and reflections on how to find true well-being. We explore the science (or lack thereof) behind popular wellness diets, the role of influencers and social-media algorithms in spreading wellness misinformation, problematic practices in the alternative- and integrative-medicine space, how wellness culture often drives disordered eating, the truth about trending topics like gut health, how to avoid getting taken advantage of when you’re desperate for help and healing, and how to care for yourself in a deeply flawed healthcare system without falling into wellness traps. *This podcast feed shares generous previews and occasional full-length episodes. To hear everything, become a paid subscriber at* *rethinkingwellness.substack.com* ( https://rethinkingwellness.substack.com/?utm_medium=podcast ) *.*

title: The End Game
author: Melissa Butterworth
description: The End Game: Laboratory Insiders on Your Side is a  podcast by senior lab executives for senior lab executives. This podcast shares valuable industry knowledge, tips, and strategies that when applied and properly executed will help lab executives succeed, thrive, and ultimately become better prepared for their own End Game.

title: Tech Tips with BSP Motorsports
author: Brandon & Chelsea Piper
description:
Welcome to Tech Tips with BSP Motorsports, where we talk cars, solve problems, and occasionally save customers from unnecessary repairs. Hosted by Brandon and Chelsea Piper, owners of BSP Motorsports in Aubrey, TX, each episode delivers practical automotive advice, maintenance insights, and real‑world knowledge to help you keep your vehicle running at its best. From daily drivers to high‑performance builds, we break down the “why” behind common issues so you can make smarter decisions about your car. Because every vehicle deserves expert care—even your everyday commuter. 


Whether you're dealing with suspension issues, alignment problems, brake concerns, mysterious noises, or that warning light you’ve been hoping would disappear, we’re here to help you understand what’s happening under the hood. From sports cars and race cars to family vehicles, our mission is simple: earn your trust and treat your vehicle like it’s our own. BSP Motorsports—where great service and expert solutions never go out of style. 

 

To learn more about BSP Motorsports visit:
https://www.BSPMotorSports.com  

BSP Motorsports  

14546 Industrial Park, Aubrey, Texas 76227 
469-731-5887

title: Talks with Hanan Iuliano
author: Hanan Iuliano
description: "Talks with Hanan Iuliano" is a podcast about life, personal growth, purpose, mindset, and meaningful conversations. Join founder and host Hanan Iuliano as she shares honest reflections, inspiring stories, and practical insights on navigating life's challenges, pursuing happiness, following your passions, embracing change, and discovering what truly matters. Each episode is designed to inspire, encourage, and remind you that every step forward counts. 🎙 New episodes every Monday & Friday at 12:00 PM AEST on all major podcast platforms.

title: The things we go through bossed up edition cherokee network
author: Cherokee dabaddest
description:
Intro of me coming back into the podcast world from radio. And filming
Also touching topics on healthy living and exercise goals

title: the director's eye
author: Someone's Favorite Productions
description:
Welcome to the director's eye. We’re looking to expand the definition of the auteur theory in a podcast that will pick a different director every season. We use their full catalog to understand the arc of their career and discuss the impact this auteur had on world cinema.

Season 1 will deep dive into the life and career of James Cameron. 9 episodes covering all of his projects leading up to the Billie Eilish concert film followed by a wrapup and summary of what we have learned along the way. 

We will drop a new episode every Wednesday. Please let us know what you think!

title: Underworld Catalog
author: Mateus Arcanjo
description:
Every nightmare begins with a simple mistake.
Welcome to the Underworld Catalog, where haunted mirrors, forbidden rituals, strange entities,
abandoned roads, and forgotten places hide just beyond reality.
Every episode is a chilling first-person horror story about curiosity, fear, and the consequences of crossing the wrong line.

title: Excel At AI In Finance
author: Mike Dion
description:
Corporate finance is breaking people. Twelve-day closes. 9pm Sunday spreadsheets. The slow drip of falling behind while every other post on your feed warns that AI is coming for your job. That’s the spot most finance pros are stuck in right now, and it doesn’t get better by working harder.
Excel at AI in Finance is the weekly podcast for finance pros who want out of the grind. Each episode runs less than your commute or coffee break and walks you through exactly how I’m using AI to automate the work that used to own my Tuesday night. Excel automation, Power BI, Python, Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT. The real tools, in the real finance jobs you run every week.

title: Weekly Dose of Aviation
author: Weekly Dose of Aviation
description: On Weekly Dose of Aviation I release episodes weekly on what is going on in the world of aviation! I plan to release weekly updates on Friday, and maybe if my podcast grows I will release video documentary episodes sometimes. I will also try to maybe get guests on in the future. (Yes I realize that I am writing this like a school essay). If you want to contact me email me at weeklydoseofaviation@outlook.com Thank you for listening!

title: The Nutrition and Health Interventions (NHi) Podcast
author: shirleykansabe
description:
NHi is a podcast for practitioners, researchers, policy makers, and anyone who's ever wondered why public health problems never seem to end. It's hosted by Shirley Kansabe, a lifelong student of health policy and systems based in Uganda.

Each episode brings evidence, field experience, and analysis of questions in nutrition and health policy in Africa. No jargon for its own sake. Just the conversations that we need but rarely have.

New episodes every two weeks.

title: Pretty Girls Love Ball
author: Né
description: 🎙️ Welcome to Pretty Girls Love Ball, the podcast where sports, culture, and conversation come together. Hosted by Né, this show covers the WNBA, NBA, NFL, game recaps, breaking news, player spotlights, trending stories, and unapologetic hot takes. Whether we’re celebrating historic performances, debating the biggest headlines, or discussing the future of sports, you’ll get authentic commentary and real passion. Because pretty girls know ball too. 👑 Pretty Faces. Elite Takes. New episodes weekly.

title: Ink and Iron History
author: Lens Flare Studios
description:
History is not just about the heroes, or the grand legends we repeat in the light. Real history is forged in the shadows—by the desperate, the broken, and the bitter. 

Welcome to Ink and Iron History. We look past the polished myths to pull back the curtain on the dark, the desperate, and the cold, hard truths left behind in the shadows of consequence. Join us for deep psychological autopsies of history's most compelling and dark timelines.

We don't believe in drawing a story out into bloated, exhausting one-hour episodes just to fill time. Instead, we break our investigations into bite-sized chunks—stripped of fluff and hyper-focused on the facts. We get right to the meat of the story, giving you a sharp, easy-to-understand narrative that delivers maximum depth in minimum time.

title: First Chair
author: Cynthia Nakao
description: Conversations with women prosecutors who are the firsts in the justice system. First chair is that seat in the courtroom where you are the lead lawyer, the decision maker, and the voice of the voiceless. In every episode you will hear from women prosecutors and their path to triumph in the courtroom, in their offices, and in their lives. Hosted by Cynthia Nakao.

title: Healthful with Norah O'Donnell
author: CBS News
description:
Healthful is the podcast that puts women’s health first. Norah brings you the doctors, the scientists, and the innovators who are at the forefront on reproductive health, menopause, cancer, and more. Forget miracle cures, vague advice, and fear-mongering and get real information you can actually use. And yes, Norah will divulge her own wellness secrets (blueberries and Botox among them).

New episodes available every Wednesday. Watch on YouTube or listen wherever you get your podcasts.

title: The Confidence Question
author: Timothy Aguilar
description: What if the most confident people you know don't actually feel confident? Dr. Tyler Hales has spent his career behind the curtain with celebrities, founders, athletes, and everyday people at the exact moment they decide to change something about themselves. And almost every one of them says the same thing before they sit in his chair: "I just want to feel more like myself." The Confidence Question is a show about that gap — between how put-together someone looks and how they actually feel. Each episode, Tyler sits down with people who've built confidence the hard way: surgeons, first responders, entrepreneurs, patients, and performers, asking the one question their story can uniquely answer. No hacks. No hype. Just honest conversations about what confidence really is, where it comes from, and how you build it when you don't feel it yet. New episodes weekly. Hosted by Dr. Tyler Hales.

title: A Working Relationship
author: Muna and Manal
description: Why does work have so much power over how we feel about ourselves? Why do so many of us struggle to rest, set boundaries, or feel like we're not enough? A Working Relationship is a podcast hosted by psychotherapists and coaches Muna and Manal, who use K-dramas to uncover the hidden beliefs and protective patterns shaping our relationship with work and ourselves. Through unforgettable stories and practical insights, you'll learn how to break free from burnout, perfectionism, people-pleasing, and money anxiety so you can build a life that feels successful, meaningful, and deeply your own.

title: We're seated
author: Karrie Kaufman and Emerald Scribner
description: We’re Seated is a laid‑back movie‑talk podcast where Emerald and Karrie and sometimes friends, dive into the films they’ve watched recently; the good, the bad, and the “why was that even made.” Each episode feels like hanging out with your friends after a movie night, breaking down scenes, characters, surprises, and whatever else comes up. If you love honest reactions, chaotic opinions, and discovering new movies to watch, pull up a seat.

title: Unpolished with Dr Heavenly
author: Dr. Heavenly Kimes
description:
Unpolished with Dr. Heavenly is where faith, facts, and fearless conversations meet.
Hosted by Dr. Heavenly Kimes—dentist, entrepreneur, and healthcare advocate—this show tackles politics, healthcare, business, relationships, and real-life issues impacting our communities.
Each episode features honest conversations with leaders, experts, and everyday voices, focused on truth, perspective, and solutions.
Our mission is to educate, empower, and inspire.
No scripts. No spin. Just Unpolished.

title: Badges & Breakthroughs Podcast
author: Dylan Cade Bates
description:
Welcome to Badges & Breakthroughs, the podcast that pulls back the curtain on the real stories of first responders, veterans, mental health advocates, and the people who love them. Step beyond the badge and past the headlines, where silence fades and truth comes out. Each week, we fearlessly dive into the lives behind the uniform, exploring unfiltered experiences, profound challenges, moments of resilience, and unexpected humor. If you’re tired of surface-level conversations and ready for honest insight into life on the front lines and the battles fought in the shadows, this show is for you. We tackle tough topics: the scars you can see and the ones you can’t, the stigma around mental health, and the breakthroughs that happen in the darkest hours. Expect gripping stories, eye-opening conversations, and a community that honors every journey. Whether you wear the badge or support someone who does, you’re invited to join us in rewriting the narrative, one breakthrough at a time. Because behind every badge is a beating heart, and every story deserves to be heard.

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/badges-breakthroughs-podcast--7182614/support.

title: Happy Girl Chats
author: Jessica Lilly
description:
The podcast made to encourage you, cheer you up & remind you of the sunny side of things!
My hope is that this podcast feels like chatting with a best friend! That it is uplifting & comforting! That it reminds you: to look for the little joys in your life, that it's always going to be okay (even when it doesn't feel like it), that you're never alone, & that there's always sunshine on cloudy days!

title: Random Access Memories
author: Jake Bernardes
description:
Technology isn't built by companies. It's built by people.

Random Access Memories (RAM) is where technology gets personal. Host Jake Bernardes sits down with founders, CISOs, engineers, operators, investors, and community builders to explore the stories behind the products, the pivots, the failures, and the moments that shaped their careers.

Whether we're talking cybersecurity, AI, startups, leadership, or life outside the office, every conversation asks the same question:

What shaped the person behind the technology?

New episodes every week.

Presented by Hubscale.

title: Sober Talk Live
author: Tom Mabe
description: How do you know if you got a drinking problem? It's easy. If it causes problems.    Sober Talk Live is a call in show, helping people who want to get sober, need to get sober or perhaps just sober curious.. whatever your reason may be we are glad you stopped by..Become a supporter of this podcast:

title: Cue the Choir
author: Brentwood Benson
description: Dive into Church Choir world with us where we meet Arrangers, Songwriters and Directors, and review new musicals, choral collections, and more for today’s church choir.

title: MBHT - Everyday People, Extraordinary Impact
author: Hosted by Liz and Porter
description:
The revolution will be podcasted.
Making Black History Today is Dallas's premier podcast and digital archive, documenting the leaders, entrepreneurs, creatives, and change-makers writing the next chapter of Black history right now. Hosted by Liz & Porter, every conversation goes deep with the voices driving impact across business, community, arts, and culture in Dallas and beyond.
This isn't history you'll read about later. You're hearing it first.
New episodes every other Thursday, follow so you never miss one.

title: Foresight with Greg Williams
author: WIRED UK
description: Designed as an extension of WIRED’s long-running live conference portfolio, these punchy, deliberate and engaging sessions reflect the same high calibre of speakers and programming featured at a WIRED event.

title: Business Built Different
author: Rachel Leslie
description: What does it look like to build a business on your own terms?   Welcome to Business Built Different, a podcast with Sisters in Solidarity in partnership with the Canadian Women's Foundation.   In this series, host Jillisa Brown sits down with women and gender diverse entrepreneurs from all over Canada to explore the realities of building and leading businesses in systems that weren't designed with them in mind. Through candid conversations about challenges, choices, and growth, we explore what feminist entrepreneurship looks like in practice and how entrepreneurs are expanding our understanding of what business can be.

title: Blending For Good
author: Blending For Good
description: Get your Vitamix and actually use it. Vitamix recipes and guidance from Blending For Good creators Shalva & Lenny Gale.

title: Saved from the Pit
author: carriewaye
description: This podcast is about my testimony of God’s mercy and Jesus’s love for us, and for learning the Bible with people who are also beginners.

title: ConstructorCast
author: AGC of America
description: AGC of America produces a monthly podcast, the ConstructorCast, a source for all the news, views, and interviews relevant to your construction business.

title: The South West Agenda with Tim Williams
author: Studio TRW
description:
A new home for politics, business and leadership conversation in the South West of England, hosted and created by Tim Williams.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Just an Average Fantasy Football Podcast
author: AVG Fantasy Football Pod
description: Just average dudes talking fantasy football

title: Turf and Timber
author: Reed and Holden
description: Two friends talking football, basketball, soccer, and whatever else is happening in the sports world. New episodes whenever we have something to say.

title: The L'Oréal Business Academy Podcast
author: L'Oréal Professional Products
description: Welcome to the L’Oréal Business Academy Podcast! Your go-to space for elevating your salon leadership. Each episode, we dive into actionable insights, industry trends, and expert strategies to help you grow your business, lead your teams, and thrive in the beauty industry.

title: Love
author: luke jarvis
description: A detail

title: Tech News Today
author: The Brass Oracle
description: Get the Latest Tech News Today, The Brass Oracle makes it easy to get tuned in.

title: Lion's Share: The Research Cast
author: Lion Share Productions
description: The Lion's Share is a podcast created by Penn State Smeal's Executive DBA students. Each episode dives into a single research paper, with two students unpacking its design, theory, and impact. Together, they explore how research informs both business scholarship and real-world leadership practice, giving listeners the lion's share of insight in every conversation.

title: Break Any Pattern
author: Termeil Hall
description:
Freedom is created by changing the way we think. 
Each episode, Termeil breaks down the patterns, mindsets, and spirituality that it takes to transform your life, relationships and purpose.

title: Not Live
author: Fox 32 Chicago
description: Not Live is a podcast is where the newsmakers stop giving soundbites and start having real conversations. We go beyond the headlines to talk about the stories, personalities, and experiences that don't fit into a two-minute TV segment. It's unscripted, unexpected, and a chance to get to know the people behind the title.

title: Guided toilet meditations from Who Gives A Crap
author: Who Gives A Crap
description: Empty your mind. Empty your bowels. Who Gives A Crap is your companion in the cubicle helping you unclench more than just your jaw. Find your rest in the restroom with these guided toilet meditations.

title: The Ambition Code
author: Julie Kellman Reading
description:
The Ambition Code is a premium podcast and conversation series about ambition, identity, career, reinvention, leadership, and the defining moments that shape who we become. Hosted by Julie Kellman Reading, the series features deeply human conversations with remarkable people who have built extraordinary lives across business, culture, creativity, entrepreneurship, leadership, and personal evolution.
At its core, The Ambition Code explores ambition as a force that moves through every part of life - how we start, how we rise, how we navigate the unexpected, and how we break through into what comes next.
Through honest and powerful conversations filled with humanity, humor, hard-earned experience, and real-world advice, the podcast gives listeners the knowledge and clarity to build momentum and move forward with confidence through every pivot, transition, risk, reinvention, and defining moment in their lives.
Built for those entering the workforce, entrepreneurs, creatives, leaders, and anyone standing at the edge of a new chapter, The Ambition Code asks the bigger questions: What would you tell the younger version of yourself? How do you keep moving when the path is unclear? What does your journey teach you about purpose, resilience, legacy, and becoming who you are meant to be?
The Ambition Code is a guide to running through open doors,  and a bold reminder that your life is waiting for you.

title: In the News with Steve Patton
author: Steve Patton
description: A weekday podcast focusing on news/sports from southeast Missouri and northeast Arkansas

title: Millionaire Matcha Podcast
author: Luxury Greg
description:
Daily Episodes 8PM EST 
Millionaire Matcha Podcast Presented By Luxury Greg
A podcast that speaks about the ups and downs of being a millionaire while enjoying the matcha of your choice.

title: Waffle Party | A Severance Podcast
author: Podcastica
description:
Hello, and welcome to this podcast listening experience. We're Jason and Karen, your designated narrative explicators. Please try to enjoy each insight, quip, and speculative comment equally.

But seriously, Severance is such a masterpiece of a show, with all its mysteries, layers of satirical commentary, wry humor, gorgeous-yet-mundane aesthetics, fun characters (played by world-class actors), and so much more. Lots to dig in to! We're so glad you decided to join us for this one. Whether you’re already a fan or it’s your first time through, we just bet it'll make you feel right as rain.

You can join in and be a part of the discussion! Write to us or send a voice message to waffleparty@podcastica.com and we’ll respond on the podcast.

Waffle Party is a part of Podcastica, where we’ve been doing episode-by-episode deep dives into amazing TV shows for well over a decade now. Check out our other fare at ⁠Podcastica.com⁠.

title: Beyond Podcast
author: Matt & Corisande Cooke
description:
What does it take to go beyond?
Welcome to Beyond, with your hosts, husband and wife duo Matt and Corisande Cooke. Expect weekly conversations that go beyond the ordinary and explore manifestation, spirituality, intuition, action, risk and what it takes to create a miraculous life.
Each episode is packed with practical manifestation tools, mindset shifts, honest stories and real techniques to help you follow your intuition, trust yourself and consciously create the life you dream about. You'll hear deep two-hander conversations between Matt and Corisande, and honest chats with guests from all walks of life who've gone beyond in their own way to create their best life.
Whether you're brand new to manifestation or ready to go deeper, this is the podcast for real conversations about what it takes.

title: St James JAX
author: St James Episcopal Church
description: Sermons and other podcasts from St. James' Episcopal Church of Jackson MS

title: Rethinking Scripture with JR Pittman
author: infofcg
description: JR Pittman was created for one purpose: to push you further than you are comfortable going. Through books, newsletters, podcasts, and an ever-expanding platform, JR Pittman exists to serve not only scholars and theologians, but everyday readers — and those who have never considered themselves readers at all. We believe the most dangerous thing a mind can do is stop questioning, and the most courageous thing a person can do is think differently than the room they're sitting in. JR Pittman is a platform for authors and thinkers who refuse to accept a conclusion simply because it has always been the accepted one — writers who challenge norms, interrogate assumptions, and invite their readers into conversations that are long overdue. Our debut title, Women Are Not the Villains, is exactly that kind of book — a work designed not to comfort the familiar, but to ignite a global conversation that the church, the scholar, and the curious mind have all been circling for centuries without the courage to land. This is only the beginning. With a growing roster of authors who think outside every box they were handed, JR Pittman is building something rare: a publishing home where expanding your thinking is not optional — it is the entire point.

title: The Monday Message with Dan Dillon
author: Dan Dillon
description:
The Monday Message with Dan Dillon is a weekly podcast about leadership, character, personal growth, and living a life of purpose.
Drawing on more than three decades in public service, Dan shares thoughtful reflections and practical lessons on resilience, accountability, integrity, and the decisions that shape who we become.
While inspired by experiences in the fire and public safety, these conversations are for anyone striving to become a better leader, spouse, parent, teammate, friend, or neighbor.
Each Monday take 10 minutes to slow down, think differently, and begin your week with purpose.
Choose the harder right over the easier wrong!

title: Clocked Out
author: Isaac green
description: Real conversations with founders investors and operators but also students employees and everyday people navigating money mindset and what it means to build a life you actually want. No scripted corporate talk.just honest breakdown of the wins failures and the moves nobody talks about

title: MJ on Air
author: Malik Scales
description: MJ On Air is my journal brought to life through conversation. Here, I share the thoughts that keep me awake, the lessons life teaches me, and the emotions that shape who I am becoming. Each episode is a reflection or a snapshot of growth, curiosity, and the ongoing journey of understanding myself and the world around me. If something I share helps you see things differently, inspires you to take a chance, or reminds you that you're capable of more than you realize, then this podcast has served its purpose. Thank you for listening. Thank you for growing with me.

title: Lernen, Führen, Gestalten: Die persönlichen Lernreisen von HSG-Alumna und -Alumni
author: HSG Alumni
description: Eine HSG-Ausbildung verändert: Denken, Handeln, Führung. Aber wie genau, und mit welcher Wirkung? Im Podcast erzählen HSG-Absolventinnen und -Absolventen ihre einzigartige Geschichte: persönlich, ehrlich und nah an der unternehmerischen Realität. Wie hat die Zeit in St. Gallen sie geprägt, und was davon konnten sie im beruflichen Umfeld umsetzen? Host Dr. Markus Ganter, HSG-Alumnus (IEMBA 01), Naturwissenschaftler und Unternehmensberater bei Lean Partners, kennt diesen Weg aus eigener Erfahrung. Seit 25 Jahren begleitet er produzierende Unternehmen durch Transformations- und Wachstumsphasen. Er spricht mit Alumni darüber, wie bei ihnen "Wissen zu Wirkung" wurde.

title: JAG Plumbing Services, Inc.
author: JAG Plumbing Services, Inc.
description:
JAG Plumbing Services is a family-owned business proudly serving Loxahatchee, FL, and surrounding areas. With decades of hands-on experience, they bring expert solutions to every job, from drain cleaning and sewer repairs to general plumbing services. Their team is known for reliability, honesty, and quality craftsmanship. Whether it's a routine fix or a major plumbing issue, they treat every customer like family and every home like their own. Jag Plumbing combines old-school work ethic with modern techniques to get the job done right the first time. Backed by generations of experience and a commitment to their community, they’ve earned a reputation as the go-to plumbers in the area for dependable, affordable service with a personal touch.

🌐 jagplumbinginc.com

title: Blue Collar Gospel Daily Bible Reading
author: Aaron Butler
description: Blue Collar Gospel Daily Bible Reading is simple Bible truth for regular folks. Monday through Friday, Rev. Aaron Butler reads one chapter of the Bible in Eastern Kentucky dialect, staying true to the meaning of the Scripture and sharing practical preaching nuggets along the way. No big words. No fancy religion. Just the Word of God explained in a plainspoken way that connects with everyday life. Grab your Bible, pour a cup of coffee, and join us one chapter at a time.

title: Sovereign Scripture: A Verse-by-Verse Journey Through the Bible
author: Brooks
description:
On the Sovereign Scripture podcast, the goal is simple: to walk through the Bible a few verses at a time, exploring the glory and majesty of Christ as revealed in His Word.
This podcast is committed to faithful, expository, verse-by-verse teaching of the Bible, because “the word of God is living and active, sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing to the division of soul and of spirit, of joints and of marrow, and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart.”
‭‭Hebrews‬ ‭4‬:‭12‬ ‭(ESV‬‬).
Scripture quotations are from The Holy Bible, English Standard Version® (ESV®). Copyright © Crossway. Used by permission. All rights reserved.

title: Soft Life, Hard Takes
author: Shania Simon
description:
This podcast is for confident, creative, ambitious people who still wrestle with self-doubt, imposter syndrome, and the fear of being judged for their opinions.
I’m here to help you build grounded, unshakeable confidence, own your voice, and stop shrinking to fit in. Through honest conversations, humour, pop culture, and real-life reflections, this podcast is a reminder that you don’t need permission or consensus to be valid.
If you’re craving community, bold energy, and a space to reconnect with your authentic self, you’re in the right place.

title: Community Checkup with Mark O’Hern
author: WVUHospitalsEast
description: Hosted by Mark O’Hern, WVU Medicine regional president, this monthly, interview-based podcast features conversational, informative discussions focused on health issues affecting the West Virginia Eastern Panhandle and surrounding region.

title: SARC Talk Journal Club
author: SARC Talk
description:
SARC Talk Journal Club is a podcast from SARC exploring the latest sarcoma research and clinical trials. Each episode features leading sarcoma experts reviewing new publications, discussing the evidence behind the findings, and exploring their impact on patient care, research, and the future of sarcoma treatment. 
Stay informed on the science driving progress in bone and soft tissue sarcomas, emerging therapies, precision medicine, and clinical trial innovation through expert conversations that connect research with real-world practice.

title: Dal Vivo
author: Chora Media - Carolina Di Domenico, Raffaele Coriglione
description: DAL VIVO è il podcast di Chora Media che racconta i concerti che hanno segnato la storia della musica in Italia. Carolina Di Domenico e Raffaele Coriglione ripercorrono cinque live leggendari, da Dalla e De Gregori a Bob Marley, Madonna, Pink Floyd e Nirvana, insieme a ospiti che li hanno vissuti da vicino: organizzatori, giornalisti, musicisti e testimoni diretti. Tra aneddoti, retroscena e polemiche dell'epoca, la serie racconta come questi eventi abbiano cambiato per sempre il modo di vivere la musica dal vivo.

title: As Seen on My Feed
author: Mayur Devman
description:
Some stories entertain us. Others quietly stay with us.
As Seen on My Feed is a storytelling podcast about books, movies, TV shows, documentaries, and remarkable ideas worth experiencing. In each episode, we take a spoiler friendly journey through one carefully chosen work, following its story, exploring its themes, and uncovering what makes it unforgettable.
We believe the best stories don't just end. They change the way we think, feel, and see the world.
Come for the story. Stay for the conversation.

title: The Marvelous Maria Podcast
author: Maria Zelaya

title: Unscripted with Jeff Pedowitz
author: The Pedowitz Group
description:
Most podcasts let their guests give the answers they came prepared to give. This one doesn’t.
Unscripted is where Jeff Pedowitz — CEO of The Pedowitz Group and one of the most recognized voices in AI-driven revenue marketing — sits down with the builders, thinkers, and leaders who are actually shaping what comes next. Not the keynote version. The real version.
Every conversation runs through the same throughline: AI is no longer the story, it’s the substrate. What does that mean for the companies being built, the policies being written, the institutions trying to hold, and the humans navigating all of it?
Guests come from everywhere — drug discovery, politics, investment, education, creative work — because the AI question doesn’t belong to any one industry. It belongs to everyone trying to do something that matters in a world that’s changing faster than the frameworks we use to understand it.
No scripts. No softballs. No prepared answers left standing.
Jeff asks what no one else thought to ask. That’s the whole show.

title: Joy in Suffering
author: Melissa Winters
description:
Are you a Christian woman living with chronic illness, chronic pain, or deep grief — wondering where God is in the middle of your suffering?

Welcome to Joy in Suffering, a Christian podcast for women navigating chronic illness, grief, anxiety, stress, and the quiet heartbreak of lost dreams.

If you’re tired of clichés like “everything happens for a reason” and long for real encouragement rooted in Scripture, honest stories, and hope that actually sustains you — you’re in the right place.

Hosted by Melissa Winters, who has lived with chronic illness since one years old. This podcast offers gentle, faith-filled conversations designed to help you:

• Anchor your joy in Christ — even in uncertainty

• Find peace in the middle of fear and worry

• Experience hope when healing feels delayed

• Feel seen, understood, and less alone

• Discover purpose in the middle of limitations

Through Stories, Scripture, and Song, each episode creates space to wrestle honestly with suffering while remembering that God is present, loving, and still working.

Whether you’re navigating long-term health struggles, grief over the life you thought you’d have, or simply a heavy season that feels overwhelming, this podcast will remind you that joy is not the absence of pain — it is the presence of Christ within it.

New episodes offer biblical encouragement, practical faith support, and compassionate conversations for Christian women who want peace in their hearts even when circumstances don’t change.

If this resonates with you, subscribe and follow the show.

Join Melissa on Instagram and Facebook for encouragement between episodes, and sign up for her Substack and email list for exclusive reflections, behind-the-scenes clips, and deeper support.

You are not alone.

Joy still remains.

title: Broadly Speaking, with Liz Solar and Betsy Billard
author: Elizabeth Solar
description:
Broadly Speaking exists because the most interesting conversations happen when curious people with different lives and the same values sit down and talk. Not at each other. Not past each other. With each other. And with you.


Every topic is a different door into the same question: What does this tell us about being human?


Liz and Betsy hold a wealth of life experience between them. Decades on commercial radio, motherhood, a Master's in Mental Health Counseling, thirty years of advising high net worth clients and managing their assets, a serious love affair with Broadway shows, an obsession with news and what makes the world tick. 


And oh yes, they do root for different major league baseball teams, but that small detail has never come between them.


They come from the same city. They ended up in different lives. They agree on almost everything that matters and cheerfully disagree on the rest. Both Bostonians by birth, Liz remains in Massachusetts. Betsy is in New York City. She moved there in 1990, thought she would try it out for a year. That year turned into 36.


The show covers everything money, culture, world events, mental health, sports, politics, Broadway, and whatever landed in their inbox from listeners this week. No topic is too big. No question is too small. No fool will be suffered.


But everyone - everyone - is welcome at the table.


Broadly speaking, that's the whole idea.

title: Audio GA4-240202501-AA2-EV01
author: Lina Peña
description:
Hola a todos, mi nombre es Lina Marcela Peña Guzmán, estudio contabilización de operaciones comerciales y financieras en el servicio nacional de aprendizaje SENA, ficha 2834467. Nuestra instructora líder es Yalile Correcha y les voy a hablar sobre la actividad de aprendizaje “Audio GA4-240202501-AA2-EV01”.
En el contexto laboral y académico, existen varias actitudes, creencias y obligaciones que son necesarias para tener éxito. A continuación, te menciono algunas de ellas: 
Actitudes: Actitud positiva: Mantener una actitud positiva frente a los desafíos y dificultades puede ayudarte a superar obstáculos y mantener la motivación.
Responsabilidad: Ser responsable implica cumplir con tus tareas y compromisos de manera puntual y eficiente. 
Creencias: Confianza en uno mismo: Creer en tus habilidades y capacidades te ayudará a enfrentar desafíos y superar obstáculos.
Persistencia: Creer en la importancia de perseverar y no rendirse fácilmente te permitirá superar dificultades y alcanzar tus metas. 
Obligaciones: Respetar las normas y reglas: Cumplir con las normas y reglas establecidas en el entorno laboral o académico es esencial para mantener un ambiente de respeto y orden.
Ser ético: Actuar de manera ética y profesional es fundamental en cualquier contexto laboral o académico.  

Estas son solo algunas de las actitudes, creencias y obligaciones necesarias en el contexto laboral y académico. Es importante recordar que cada entorno puede tener sus propias particularidades, por lo que es necesario adaptarse y cumplir con las expectativas específicas de cada situación.
